title: NEW DATE: The Noodle Road: From Asia to Europe to America - Talk and Tasting NEW
location: Montclair Main - Conference Room, Montclair Public Library
description:
NEW CLASS DATE DUE TO WEATHER ON 2/23

This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

The event highlights an engaging food experience that explores how noodles have traveled across continents and connected cultures throughout history. Led by nutrition professionals, this session blends food history, hands-on learning, and delicious tasting.

We will watch a short video, followed by a lecture and an interactive talk covering the story of noodles—where they originated, how noodle-making traditions spread along ancient trade routes, and how Eastern and Western noodle styles influenced each other. You will also discover different kinds of noodles from around the world and learn what makes each type unique.

Next, we will discuss how noodles can be a nutritious choice by sharing tips on making healthier noodle-based meals, what to look for when choosing noodles (such as whole grains, ingredients, and sodium content), and how to balance noodles with vegetables and proteins. The event will conclude with a live cooking demonstration of Korean hand-pulled noodles and fresh pasta made from scratch, followed by a tasting. Ingredient lists will be provided on the day to ensure the safety of participants with food allergies or sensitivities.

title: Iftar dinner with Peace Islands at FCC, 6pm in the Guild Room -Please RSVP
location: 40 S Fullerton Ave, Montclair, NJ 07042, USA
description:
Enter from the back parking lot. 

Experience this special Ramadan tradition through an evening of education, friendship and food!

The holy month of Ramadan is a sacred time for Muslims. Similar to our season of Lent, it is an time of intentional prayer,actions and fasting to bring our souls closer to God. One of the daily practices is to fast from sun up to sun down each day and then break the fast with an Iftar dinner -- a celebratory time to gather with friends and family ingratitude for God's blessings.

On March 5, our friends from Peace Islands Institute, have offered to provide a special experience to share this tradition with us. They will offer a presentation on the spiritual and cultural practices of Ramadan and provide a delicious traditional Turkish Iftar dinner for us.  As always, we will enjoy deepening our friendships with this wonderful community! 

IF YOU'LL BE ATTENDING PLEASE RSVP ON THE LINK BELOW AS SOON AS POSSIBLE. Peace Islands need to know how many to cook for.

IF YOU'D LIKE TO BRING A DESSERT: What we need are bite-sized finger desserts (no gelatin or alcoholic ingredients.)  SIGNUP HERE

description:
Members of Temple Ner Tamid are invited to a home-hosted Iftar dinner with members of the Peace Island Institute
on Thursday, March 5 at 5:45pm in homes throughout Northern New Jersey. 
Iftar is the evening meal that breaks the daily fast during Ramadan, a month when Muslims fast from dawn to sunset
as a spiritual practice centered on reflection, gratitude, and compassion. 
Guests will arrive shortly before sunset and begin by breaking the fast together, followed by a shared dinner.
The evening will include a brief explanation of Ramadan and plenty of time for relaxed conversation and connection. 
Sign Up Here and We Will Match You With the Right Dinner

"And they devoted themselves to the apostles' teaching and the fellowship, to the breaking of bread and the prayers. And awe came upon every soul, and many wonders and signs were being done through the apostles. And all who believed were together and had all things in common. And they were selling their possessions and belongings and distributing the proceeds to all, as any had need. And day by day, attending the temple together and breaking bread in their homes, they received their food with glad and generous hearts, praising God and having favor with all the people. title: Eagle Rock Reservation
description:
Let's Walk!!
JOIN US FOR A WALK. WE WALK AT A MODERATE PACE ON UNPAVED PATHS.

We will hike for about an hour - 3.5 miles. We will be walking on trails in the woods, so appropriate footwear, long pants & socks, and bug spray.

Will will meet at:
[https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7](https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7) Eagle Rock Trail Parking Lot by Kiosk#1

If you enter from Eagle Rock Way, go pass the Highlawn and stay left. There will be a parking lot at the end of the curve to your right.

Eagle Rock Reservation is 408 acres of hills, streams, and valleys located on the borders of West Orange, Verona, and Montclair, New Jersey. Though located in the middle of the most densely populated county in the most densely populated state in the country, the Reservation is mostly undeveloped and boasts an extensive trail system within its borders.

**Please make sure to RSVP and arrive 5 minutes prior to start time.**

FOLLOW US ON INSTAGRAM - letswalkmeetup

Disclaimer: If you join us for a hike, please understand that you are responsible for your own preparedness and well-being and will hold no one else liable in case of injury or mishap. Event will be canceled in the event of extreme weather.

title: Let's Walk!! Orange Reservoir Loop
description:
Let's Walk!!
**DUE TO TRAIL CONDITIONS AT EAGLE ROCK THE EVENT FOR FRIDAY HAS BEEN MOVED TO ORANGE RESERVOIR**

**WE START ON TIME SO PLEASE ARRIVE 5 MINUTES EARLY.**

Join us for a walk at the Orange Reservoir Loop located near Turtle Back Zoo.

The loop is a 1.8-mile paved trail around the reservoir. This is a very popular area for birding, running, and walking. The walk is for all skill levels.

You can park at McLoone's Boathouse and we will meet in the lot at the trail entrance. Please dress for the weather, wear appropriate shoes, bring water, and a friendly disposition.

Rain or extreme weather will cancel the event.

Disclaimer: If you join us for a hike, please understand that you are responsible for your own preparedness and well-being and will hold no one else liable in case of injury or mishap.

title: Tulip Springs Hike w/ Donna ~5 miles - LOCATION CHANGE!
description:
South Mountain Conservancy Activities Meetup Group
**WEATHER PERMITTING**
Meet 10:00 at Tulip Springs new parking lot. 5 Mile hike staying on that side of the Reservation. Bring snack water poles and **spikes** if needed.

**Leader**: Donna Burkat- 973-454-5635 call or text if you need to cancel.

\*\*\* In registering for this or any other South Mountain Conservancy (SMC) programs in 2026 online, I hereby release and agree to hold the SMC, its volunteers, staff and Board of Trustees, harmless from and waive on behalf of myself, my heirs, and any personal representatives any and all causes of action, claims, demands, damages, costs, expenses and compensation for damage or loss to myself and/or property that may be caused by any act, or failure to act, in this SMC activity, or that may otherwise arise in any way in connection with any other SMC activity. I understand that this release discharges the SMC from any liability or claim that I, my heirs, or any personal representatives, may have against the SMC with respect to any bodily injury, illness, death, medical treatment, or property damage that may arise from, or in connection to, any SMC program. This liability waiver and release extends to the SMC along with Essex County and its staff and any other affiliated organization or SMC partner. I also understand that by signing below I approve the possible use of photos of me at this SMC event on social media or in promotional materials.
**BY REGISTERING FOR THIS HIKE I attest I am in good health and attest that I: Am not experiencing any symptoms of illness such as cough, shortness of breath or difficulty breathing, fever, chills, repeated shaking with chills, muscle pain, headache, sore throat, or new loss of taste or smell.**
**Am following all CDC recommended guidelines, above, and limiting my exposure to the novel coronavirus/COVID-19.**
\# Arrival\, Weather\, & Cancellations
Please come to the starting point 10 minutes ahead of time so the hike can start promptly. Times are our best estimates; they can vary depending on conditions, and the size and pace of a group. If you have any questions, call the leader. **Temperatures above 90 degrees and steady rain cancels, but intermittent drizzle does not. You will *not* be notified.** **Bring water. Wear hiking boots, preferably waterproof with good soles and traction devices and bring poles (optional).** Dogs are not allowed on hikes unless specified.
These hikes are publicized first to SMC members and then advertised to the public one week before a hike. There is a limit of 25 participants, so pre-registration is required at least two days before a hike to sign the liability waiver online and allow lists to be sent to hike leaders. If you have registered and then find you cannot attend, please cancel your order online. **Repeated no shows without prior cancellation will result in removal from list.**
**To become a current member visit [[https://www.somocon.org/membership/](https://www.somocon.org/membership/)]([https://www.somocon.org/membership/](https://www.somocon.org/membership/)).**

title: CALI SCHOOL OF MUSIC | Atlantic Brass Quintet Masterclass
description:
Atlantic Brass Quintet MasterclassFREE and Open to the PublicReservations not required
The Atlantic Brass Quintet performs the uniquely American music of composer Jeff Scott, and will coach Cali School brass ensembles in a masterclass setting.
Widely acclaimed as one of the world's finest and most versatile brass chamber ensembles, the Atlantic Brass Quintet has performed in 48 of the United States and dozens of countries across four continents. Atlantic specializes in masterful and vibrant presentations of repertoire spanning five centuries and a broad spectrum of styles, from artistic transcription of baroque music to the lively music of Balkan brass bands. Winner of six international chamber music competitions, the Quintet's distinctive sound, impeccable ensemble, stunning virtuosity, and warm, inviting stage presence have won praise from scores of critics.

Hosted by: The College of the Arts

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12228315

title: Be Internet Awesome: Internet Safety For Kids (2nd-5th Graders)
location: Montclair Main - Storytime Room, Montclair Public Library
description:
In today's digital landscape, children need more than just access -- they need guidance, values, and tools to navigate it responsibly. That's where Be Internet Awesome comes in! In this program series, they'll learn the five Pillars: Smart, Alert, Strong, Kind, and Brave. These Pillars teach kids to think critically, protect their privacy, stand up to cyberbullying, and build a culture of respect and empathy online. 

Parents are encouraged to join our sessions. We will have some take-home materials available after each session to continue the learning at home! 

This session will cover the pillar: STRONG

What the kids will learn: Kids learn how to create strong passwords and keep private information safe from hackers and prying eyes.

title: Yoga @ the Library (Tweens & Grownups)
location: Montclair Main - Auditorium, Montclair Public Library
description:
Wrap up the week with a refreshing yoga session for tweens and their grownups! (No unaccompanied grownups, please.) Instructor Brittany Martorella will lead you through gentle yoga poses and breathing exercises to help achieve inner peace. Registration required.

Participants should wear socks and bring their own bath/beach towels or yoga mats.

Meet your instructor:
Brittany Martorella is a seasoned yoga teacher with over a decade of experience honing her craft. Originally certified by Yoga to the People in NYC, Brittany's journey has led her to share her expertise internationally, from the bustling streets of NYC and Brooklyn to the serene landscapes of Costa Rica, where she studied and taught at Peace Retreat. Specializing in Vinyasa, Brittany's classes offer a dynamic blend of movement and breath, invigorating the body and soothing the soul. Her love for restorative postures provides students with a chance to deeply relax and rejuvenate.
With her infectious laughter, she loves to remind her students that yoga is meant to be fun. She creates an atmosphere of light-heartedness and levity, setting the stage for true calm and zen focus in every class. Join Brittany on the mat and let's laugh, flow, and grow together!

title: Department of Theatre and Dance | Passage '26
description:
TICKETS: $25 |$30 includes a $5 per ticket handling fee when ordering online
A fantasia inspired by E.M. Forster’s novel A Passage to India, PASSAGE is set in the fictional Country X, which is a neocolonial client of Country Y. A local doctor from Country X and a visiting teacher from Country Y begin to forge a friendship that is challenged after a fateful trip to a local attraction, exposing the complexities of cultural misunderstanding and power imbalances. A meditation on colonization, the mutability of political borders, and the elusive nature of true understanding.
This production of Passage is recommended for ages 16+ for mature themes and profanity.LATE SEATING - DISCRETION OF THE HOUSE MANAGER

Featuring Dr. Monica Taylor and Dr. Emily Klein from Montclair State University + filmmakers Chris Heinze and Dominique Debroux 
Presented by: The Gender, Sexuality, and Women’s Studies Department at Montclair State University and Planned Parenthood of Metropolitan New Jersey

title: Erev Shabbat Service
description:
Erev Shabbat services begin on Friday at 6:30pm. These services are especially musical, often with a band of congregants accompanying the Cantor. Usually around an hour, these services are filled with a lot of spirit. It’s not uncommon to see people dancing in their seat or hear the whole congregation clapping their hands. Friday night is intergenerational. Glance around and see senior citizens sitting beside religious school families both getting something out of the services.  
In the summer months, services are led by members of the congregation with support from our clergy.
Join us most weeks from anywhere for Erev Shabbat Services through Zoom or our YouTube Livestream.

title: SHOOP! Special House Edition w/ Aaron Dae (Razor-N-Tape NYC) & Cher La Luna
location: Porta Montclair, 499 Bloomfield Ave, Montclair, NJ, 07042, United States
description:
A return to 90s house—deep grooves, timeless energy, and a dancefloor built for movement.
SHOOP returns on March 6th with a special all–90s house music edition, created as a tribute to the sound and spirit of the era that shaped modern dancefloors. 
This night is about deep grooves, soulful rhythms, and the timeless energy of 90s house music. Expect carefully selected records, long blends, and a room built for movement—music rooted in history and played with intention. 
Joining the night is Aaron Dae, making this edition of SHOOP feel like a full-circle moment. With shared roots in the underground house scene and decades of experience between them, the night brings together memory, atmosphere, and a deep respect for the dancefloor. 
This is a space for dancers, listeners, and those who know the feeling. 
Special Guest:
Aaron Dae (Razor-N-Tape Brooklyn)
Aaron Dae has been a driving force in underground dance music since the 1990s. A DJ, promoter, and label owner, he is the co-head of Razor-N-Tape, the influential NYC label and Greenpoint BK record store known for its deep connection to disco and house culture. 
Aaron’s sound blends deep house, disco, funk, and soul—grounded in classic dancefloor traditions and refined through decades behind the decks. 
Resident DJ
Cher La Luna aka Miss Merenda
DJ Cher La Luna is a selector shaped by the house music scenes of Philadelphia and NYC, with roots in the 90s club and rave era. Her sets are groove-driven and soulful, built around feeling, flow, and dancefloor connection. Through her SHOOP party series, she creates nights centered on music with history, warmth, and intention.

title: Inner Body Meditation
description:
The Mindful Stream
"Awareness of the inner body takes you beyond the mind." - Eckhart Tolle

In this mediation session we will bring our attention here: to the inner body. Sessions are suitable for newcomers, those experienced in Tolle's teachings and anyone wishing to remain completely anonymous. They will last around 20 minutes and cameras and microphones will be disabled.

(Depending on the situation, a free discussion may be held at the end with mics and cameras allowed.)

See you there!

Emrys also organises a free Work of Byron Katie online mediation group. Visit [this Meetup Group link](https://www.meetup.com/thework-runsfree/) for more information.

title: Creative Coding Foundations (Early Elementary)
location: RecRoom, 1 Lackawanna Plaza, Ste. 6, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Creative Coding Foundations is a hands-on introduction to coding for kindergarten through 2nd grade, designed specifically for early readers and developing learners. 
These drop-in friendly workshops follow a consistent, kid-friendly structure to help young learners feel confident and successful: 
• Concept: a short, interactive introduction to one simple coding idea
• Practice: guided exploration using visual, beginner-friendly tools
• Open Build: time to create, experiment, and try ideas independently with instructor support 
Every session also includes age-appropriate lessons on digital citizenship and online safety, helping kids learn how to be thoughtful, safe, and responsible technology users from the start. 
The focus is on curiosity, creativity, and building confidence with technology—no prior coding experience required. 
Details:
• Short, hands-on workshop
• Small-group format
• Drop-in friendly
• Designed for early elementary learners
• No prior coding experience required 
Community Sponsorship:
20% of seats are reserved for community sponsorship. Families interested in sponsored spots can email info@kineticmindscreative.com
.

title: Verona Park
description:
Let's Walk!!
JOIN US FOR A WALK

We will meet at the benches in front of the boathouse.

We will walk the loop around the park. The goal will be at least three miles, but there is plenty of opportunity to stop or continue to fit your personal goals.

We will be walking on a paved surface.

We walk at a medium speed.

Please plan to arrive 5 minutes **early** so that we can **start on time.**

Verona Park is 54.32 acres with a lake running through it. It is bordered by Lakeside and Bloomfield Avenues in the southern part of Verona. It is the fifth largest park in the Essex County system, making it a great place to walk.

Disclaimer: If you join us for a hike, please understand that you are responsible for your own preparedness and well-being and will hold no one else liable in case of injury or mishap. Event will be canceled in the event of extreme weather.

title:
👠From Heels to Boots - IWD Hike w/Ivette ~ 5-6 miles🥾
for 50+ Community
description:
South Mountain Conservancy Activities Meetup Group
**50+ hike celebrating International Women's Day - open to both, women and men.**

**We aren't just hitting the trails for the views; we are walking with the intention of celebrating the incredible milestones women have achieved across every field.**
_This hike is to remind us of women’s resilience and the struggle for women’s rights to the uphill trail, sometimes rocky, requiring endurance, but offering a better perspective of the **“the higher you go”.**_

All participants will receive a ribbon to honor a special lady in their life. We will flag the trail with Purple Ribbons in recognition of the Women’s History Month in USA.
The flags will stay on for the duration of the month for visitors to follow them from Tulip Springs to Hemlock Falls Over the Girl Scout bridge and back to Tulip Springs via Turtleback trail.

This is a social event so a steady, but moderate pace will be maintained to enjoy nature and carry a conversation.• Must be able to walk 6 miles.• No dogs please.• Times are our best estimates, but can vary depending on conditions, and the size or pace of the group.

**What to bring for a Hike:** Ice Spikes, Water, Gatorade, Hiking Shoes or Boots (no sneakers), Snacks, Trekking Poles (optional), Bug Spray, Sunscreen, and a Talking Point! Dress for the weather and in Light Layers. Steady rain or snow cancels the hike.

**NOTE:** You are responsible for bringing appropriate gear, supplies, clothing, necessary medication, EpiPen and first aid kit.

**\*****\*****\* In registering for this or any other South Mountain Conservancy (SMC) programs in 2026 online, I hereby release and agree to hold the SMC, its volunteers, staff and Board of Trustees, harmless from and waive on behalf of myself, my heirs, and any personal representatives any and all causes of action, claims, demands, damages, costs, expenses and compensation for damage or loss to myself and/or property that may be caused by any act, or failure to act, in this SMC activity, or that may otherwise arise in any way in connection with any other SMC activity. I understand that this release discharges the SMC from any liability or claim that I, my heirs, or any personal representatives, may have against the SMC with respect to any bodily injury, illness, death, medical treatment, or property damage that may arise from, or in connection to, any SMC program. This liability waiver and release extends to the SMC along with Essex County and its staff and any other affiliated organization or SMC partner. I also understand that by signing below I approve the possible use of photos of me at this SMC event on social media or in promotional materials.
**BY REGISTERING FOR THIS HIKE I attest I am in good health and attest that I: Am not experiencing any symptoms of illness such as cough, shortness of breath or difficulty breathing, fever, chills, repeated shaking with chills, muscle pain, headache, sore throat, or new loss of taste or smell.**
Am following all CDC recommended guidelines, above, and limiting my exposure to the novel coronavirus/COVID-19.
\# Arrival\, Weather\, & Cancellations
Please come to the starting point 10 minutes ahead of time so the hike can start promptly. Times are our best estimates; they can vary depending on conditions, and the size and pace of a group. If you have any questions, call the leader.
- Temperatures above 90 degrees and steady rain/snow cancels, but intermittent drizzle does not. **You will not be notified.** Bring water. Wear hiking boots, preferably waterproof with good soles and traction devices and bring poles (optional).
- Dogs are not allowed on hikes unless specified.
These hikes are publicized first to SMC members and then advertised to the public one week before a hike. There is a limit of 25 participants, so pre-registration is required at least two days before a hike to sign the liability waiver online and allow lists to be sent to hike leaders. If you have registered and then find you cannot attend, please cancel your order online.
**Repeated no shows without prior cancellation will result in removal from list.**

**To become a current member visit [[https://www.somocon.org/membership/](https://www.somocon.org/membership/)](https://www.somocon.org/membership/).**

title: Wisdom Walkshops (Zoom)
description:
Street Wisdom Walkshops were created by Dr. Marcia McFee, creator of “The Way” Lenten series.  The WalkShops take place on ZOOM and are facilitated by Dr. McFee as a “way of wandering, meandering, and keeping our eyes open for clues to help us reflect and discern about next steps.”  These WalkShops are open to those who want to walk outside or those who want to take part from the comfort of their home.  Saturdays In Lent through March 28. 
  
For more information: worshipdesignstudio.com/walkshops

title: Scout Program - Animal Program
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Scout Program – Animal Program 
Saturday March 7th 
Times: 1:15-2:15 & 2:30-3:30 
  
Description: Scouts looking to learn about animals and their habitats look no further and join us for an afternoon meeting live animals from Eyes of the Wild Exotic Animal Rescue. An Animal Educator will share fascinating facts about where these animals live and what makes each one so unique. Topics discussed at this event relate to animal themed badges for various girl scout levels. This program is held indoors, with an outdoor self-guided scavenger hunt before or after the program (weather permitting).  
Fee: $10 per scout, $5 per adult chaperone. Minimum 2 adult chaperones per troop.  
Registration Information: 
-Reservation requests open on February 11th at 9 AM. 
-Registration by Troop Leaders only – No individual scout registrations 
To request a spot for your troop: 
1 – Complete Information by clicking on the link below starting  on February 11th at 9 AM. 
2 – Payment link will be emailed to you as space allows on a first come first serve basis.

title: 3D Origami Workshop
location: The Eclectic Chic Boutique, 547 Bloomfield Avenue, Montclair, NJ, 07042, United States
description:
Take your folding skills to the next dimension! Unlike traditional origami, 3D origami (also known as Golden Venture folding) involves assembling many small, interlocking “units” to create sturdy, intricate sculptures. Our instructor will walk you through step by step to help you enjoy this deeply rewarding blend of paper folding and 3D puzzle building.

title: "Bloom Where You Are Planted" musical evening, March 7, 7pm at FCC
description:
FCC Member and professional singer, Daniel Belnavis, will offer a concert event centering on finding community and belonging in our anxiety-ridden,too-often-isolating world. Hope, as well as resistance, will be the through-line of the evening's music.  You can register here!

Admission for the event is free, however donations are much appreciated! Proceeds will benefit both The FCC Olmsted Garden and the American Friends Service Committee's New Jersey Immigrant Rights Program in Newark.  
 
You can donate to the "Bloom Where You Are Planted" fund HERE or in person on the night of the event.

title: Bloom Where You are Planted: An Evening of Hopeful Songs
location: First Congregational Church of Montclair, 40 S. Fullerton Avenue, Montclair, NJ, 07042, United States
description:
Bloom Where You Are Planted: An Evening of Hopeful Songs is a concert event conceived by singer, writer, and board-certified music therapist, Daniel James Belnavis. The night will consist of songs and stories by Belnavis, a Montclair, NJ native, whose performance credits include Hamilton: An American Musical (Angelica Tour); Dreamgirls (Japanese Tour); and Violet (Boston’s SpeakEasy Stage Company). 
​Hope, as well as resistance, will be the through-line of the evening’s music, with Belnavis singing an array of covers of uplifting songs, both popular and lesser known, that speak to the harrowing times we are living through. Belnavis’s wish is for Bloom to serve as a container that provides a sense of calm, understanding, respite, and, most importantly, community in the midst of turbulent times. 
Bloom will be held in the stunning sanctuary of First Congregational Church of Montclair, an open and affirming church, with a rich history rooted in abolition and inclusion. 
​Admission for the event is free, however donations are much appreciated! Proceeds will benefit both The FCC Olmsted Garden and the American Friends Service Committee’s New Jersey Immigrant Rights Program in Newark.

title: Passaic Watershed Series: Great Falls, Goffle, and More!
description:
Metrotrails: NJ, PA, NY Metropolitan Area Hiking and More!
Join us for this point to point hike in our Passaic River Watershed series, this time leading us along a tributary known as the Goffle Brook, which has some great trails along it, as well as on back streets and through parks along the river itself. Featured will be the Great Falls National Historic Site and some of the historic structures around it.
We'll continue to the west through the city and visit other small parks, probably get some food, and head to West Falls.
Distance will be about 15 miles. If needed, contact me at 908 343 8374

title: In-Person and Live Stream Service
description:
Sunday worship takes place at 10am in person at 176 Cooper Avenue in Montclair or livestreamed on our YouTube channel:  Sunday Worship Livestream 
Union Congregational Church is located in Montclair, New Jersey, at 176 Cooper Avenue (nearest major cross streets are Grove Street and Valley Road). There is street parking on Cooper Avenue. We have a small parking lot for seniors and those with limited mobility reached through a driveway to the left of the church. There are two accessible spots and a ramp on the left side of the building. Additional parking is available on neighboring streets. Please note that Cooper Avenue runs one way on Sundays, so it is best to enter from Valley Road or Northview Avenue. 
Enter the church through the main Sanctuary doors under the tower or through the accessible entrance through the Assembly Room at the top of the ramp on the left side of the church. 
Join Us Here   
Click here for the Sunday Worship Bulletin

title: La Golondrina: Children's Musician & Author Sonia De Los Santos! (All ages welcome)
location: Montclair Main - Auditorium, Montclair Main - Cafe Area
description: On the porch of her grandmother's home, a girl watches a migratory swallow swoop and dart, delighting in its song and imagining the journey it will make–much like the one her papá is making, and that she will soon make too. Based on her popular song, Latin Grammy nominee Sonia De Los Santos's debut picture book is a joyful celebration of home, family, and the journeys that shape our lives and communities. De Los Santos reads La Golondrina, followed by a performance of her song! Books will be available for purchase and signing. Registration recommended for updates and reminders.

title: Building Montclair in LEGO
location: Montclair Art Museum, 3 South Mountain Ave, Montclair, NJ, 07042, United States
description:
Join architect Michael Schwartz of Building Blocks Workshops for a hands-on family session using over 100,000 LEGO™ bricks. Families learn basic architectural principles as they recreate Montclair landmarks such as the Museum, the library, and historic homes. Pick a favorite local structure to build together as a scale model. At the end of the session, each family places their building on a large-scale map to create a shared view of our community. 
Co-presented with Montclair History Center in conjunction with Building Blocks Workshops. 
Building Montclair in LEGO™ has two sessions to choose from. If our 1–3 p.m. session is full, check our 4–6 p.m. session here.

title: Confirmation Class (Union Cong and Glen Ridge Cong)
Lenape Way Walks with Creation Justice Team (Offsite)
description: As part of our Lenten journey, we will hike some familiar and lesser-known parts of the Lenape Trail. It is not as old, long, well-known or as crowded as the Camino de Santiago in Spain, but parts of the trail are in Montclair and Bloomfield: Mills Reservation, the Iris Gardens, Tuers Park, Brookdale Park and Clark’s Pond. Join us on our walk together as we connect to ourselves, each other, and to the Lenape people indigenous to this land.

title: Open Book, Open Mind; Nicholas Boggs, "Baldwin: A Love Story"
description:
Registration for this event is FULL. Please come to the auditorium at 3:30 p.m. to get on the standby list. We'll start admitting standby guests at 4:05 p.m. 

Author Nicholas Boggs will be talking about his instant New York Times bestseller, "Baldwin: A Love Story," with Rachel Swarns ("The 272"), an associate professor at NYU's journalism school. “Lively and vigorously researched . . . Boggs has dug much deeper than his predecessors . . . "Baldwin: A Love Story" is superlative, and it should become the new gold standard for Baldwin studies.” ―Los Angeles Times



Doors open at 3:30 p.m. The discussion will be followed by an audience Q&A and book sales and signing with Watchung Booksellers. 

Love James Baldwin? Click here to register for the Everything Book Club discussion of "Giovanni's Room" two days later, on Tuesday, March 10, at 1:15 p.m. 

Open Book / Open Mind is sponsored by Montclair Public Library Foundation, Watchung Booksellers, Anonymous, David and Mary Lee Jones, and our individual Underwriters. We are also grateful for the generous support of our in-kind sponsors, First Congregational Church of Montclair, The George, and Amanti Vino. To support Open Book / Open Mind and other library programs, click here to donate.
source: Montclair Local News, Montclair Public Library

title: Beat the Sunday Scaries with BEAM
location: 153 Park St, Montclair, NJ 07042, 153 Park St., Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Is work getting you down? Do you dread going back to your job on Mondays, or any day? 
Whether you’re stuck in the grind, just entering the workforce, recently laid off, or struggling to land your dream role, you’re not alone! 
Please join us on Sunday, March 8th, 3–6pm in Montclair, to beat the “Sunday Scaries” – that sinking feeling in our stomach as Monday approaches. We invite you to drop by for a friendly group to say hi and connect. 
This is not a hiring event – and is not affiliated with any employer or agency. We’re just local community members looking to boost and strengthen one another. 
This is not a “Hello, My Name Is” networking event. We’re serious about helping one another build real connections, figuring out how we can support one another, getting through the work week together, and navigating the “work cost” of living. 
We’ll tell stories and ask each other questions about work and life. We’ll play an icebreaker game for fun. We’ll have a laid-off HR recruiter, in case anyone wants insider tips. 
Get off Linkedln and come meet others IRL – in real life!
– Bond with other Essex County folks who face similar work issues.
– Chat about beating the corporate grind, transitioning careers, working parenthood, unemployment, job survival – or whatever work+life topics are on your mind.
– Ask about tips for resumes, job searching, interviewing, seeking a raise, and salary negotiations. 
Even though this meeting is located at a church, we’re completely nonreligious and aren’t looking to push an agenda – just mutual support, maybe ongoing in future meetings if participants want. All are welcome – working or not, any industry, any age, etc. 
Location: 153 Park Street in Montclair, entrance on the right-hand side of the First Lutheran Church building, near Montclair High School. Parking is free. Refreshments will be served! Please RSVP online at tinyurl.com/beam-scaries ! 
BEAM (Building Essex Area Mutuality, beamnj.org) is a group of community members in the Essex County, NJ area who organize mutual support, aid, education, and community action to meet needs, grow connections, and challenge systems of exploitation and harm.

title: In Bloom: An International Women's Day Workshop at the MC Hotel
location: THE MC HOTEL, 690 BLOOMFIELD AVE, MONTCLAIR, NJ, 07042, United States
description:
Get into your body
We will begin with a mini yoga stretch session to help you arrive, soften, and wake up your presence. Expect gentle stretches, simple movement, and space to settle into yourself before the rest of the experience unfolds. 
Get into your inner world
You will be guided through a metta meditation, giving you time to pause, reflect, and reconnect with yourself in a supportive and grounded way. 
Get into your community
We will take part in an interactive workshop and shared conversation designed to deepen self-love, build connection, and remind you that you are not alone in what you are moving through. 
Get into the beauty of nature
You will receive a bouquet from Sophie’s Garden to take home, a tangible reminder of growth, intention, and care. 
All of this takes place in the stunning Gatsby Room at the MC Hotel, with sweeping views of New York City. A setting that invites you to slow down, expand, and feel inspired. 🌸

title: Mah Jongg: Open Play
location: Montclair Main - Cafe Area, Montclair Public Library
description:
PLEASE NOTE: You must sign up for EACH INDIVIDUAL SESSION. Signing up for one session does not register you for any following classes. Open Play is open on a first come, first served basis. 

Open Play is the perfect setting for players who know the basics and want to enjoy friendly games, practice their skills, and build confidence at the table. We'll bring the tiles, and all the accessories.  You bring your 2025 National Mah Jongg League card and your love of the game! The focus is on learning to play independently, building camaraderie, and having fun. Come meet fellow Mah Jongg enthusiasts, sharpen your strategies, and enjoy the lively spirit of the game!

Students must know the basics of mah jongg.  If you're new to the game, or don't feel comfortable sitting at a table and playing with others at a similar level, please register for a Beginner class by clicking here. Skip dates are Feb. 9th and 16th.

 

PLEASE NOTE:


	Students should have taken Beginner Mah Jongg or have equivalent knowledge and experience. 


Students should bring the 2025 National Mah Jongg League card to Open Play.

title: Memory Café - A Dementia-Friendly Social Gathering
description:
For adults with dementia and their caregivers, this event offers a welcoming, safe, calming space for socializing, music, art and reminiscing every month.


A “Memory Café” is a dementia-friendly social gathering for individuals with early-stage memory loss or mild cognitive decline and their care givers.

The meetings will be held in a safe and comfortable environment where people can interact and socialize with others having similar experiences. At the gatherings participants can get support in a non-judgmental setting, while enjoying the benefits of music for the brain.

The meetings will be run by Lisy Espindola, LCSW and Kim McLennon, MSW and music therapist. Lunch and/or snacks will be served.

This is a joint program between Lifelong Montclair at the Township of Montclair and the Montclair Public Library.

Attendance is free but registration is required. Questions? Call 973-744-3278.

To stay informed, sign up for one or all of our eNewsletters, click here.

Photographs may be taken during our programs and events for promotional purposes such as social media and e-newsletters. If you do not wish to have your photograph taken please let us know before the event.

title: Clinical Psychology (MA) Virtual Information Session
description:
Transform Lives With A Career InClinical Psychology
Montclair's Clinical Psychology program combines advanced education with practical experience. Whether you’re interested in working in private practice or research, we’ll walk you through the wide range of career paths available for clinical psychology graduates.We offer two STEM-Designated concentrations within our rigorous Master's program, tailored to your unique practice objectives:

Clinical Psychology, Child/Adolescent Psychology Concentration (MA)Students in the child/adolescent concentration learn about the unique psychological needs of children and adolescents, as well as developmental considerations in psychopathology, assessment, and treatment, including the role of family and social contexts. This concentration is designed to prepare students for MA-level clinical positions in mental health settings, particularly those that serve children and families.  
Clinical Psychology, Forensic Psychology Concentration (MA)The Forensic Psychology concentration is designed to prepare students to offer master’s level clinical services to legal system-involved populations in settings that do not require licensure. Such settings include mental health and drug court settings, offender rehabilitation programs, child and family advocacy settings and bona fide public service agencies exempted by state boards of psychological examiners for non-doctoral providers.

Ready to learn more?Join us for a virtual information session on March 9 at 5:30 PM (EST) to explore Montclair's programs in Clinical Psychology and speak directly with our faculty. 

Hosted by: The Office of Graduate Admissions

Online Location: https://graduate.montclair.edu/register/?id=59e7f224-9d6f-421d-bbd5-b9e950bc88b2

description:

This class is for those of you who know little to no Spanish and are interested in acquiring some practical communication skills. You will be introduced to basic greetings and practice useful questions and answers. You will learn the fundamentals of Spanish pronunciation while mastering introductory grammar and vocabulary.

Topics covered:


	conversation/questions
	greetings
	personal information
	expressions of courtesy
	feelings
	asking for direction
	talk about needs and want
	where people are from.
	Alphabet
	Numbers/Days/date
	Colors
	Basic commands
	Some ar/er/ir  regular verbs
	Verb to be: ser/estar
	Verb to go: ir
	Verb Tener/Necesitar/Querer
	Ir   a
	Negation
	Subject pronouns/difference between Tú/Usted
	Gender and number of nouns/definite/indefinite articles
	Some adjectives including cuánto/a
	The expression “Hay”
	Basic geography, culture and food.

title: Everything Book Club, "Giovanni's Room" by James Baldwin

description:
Don't miss the Library's Open Book / Open Mind program with author Nicholas Boggs about his bestselling new biography "Baldwin: A Love Story" on Sunday, March 8, two days before book club!  In our discussion, we'll talk about Boggs's conversation as well as Baldwin's brilliant classic about a young American expat caught between conventional morality and his true desires in 1950s Paris. If you've always meant to read this book, now's your chance! If you've read it before, let's explore it again together in the context of Baldwin's own life. 

“Exciting…. A book that belongs in the top rank of fiction.” —The Atlantic

James Baldwin was an essayist, playwright, novelist and voice of the American civil rights movement known for works including 'Notes of a Native Son,' 'The Fire Next Time' and 'Go Tell It on the Mountain.'

title: C3Workplace/C3Worx presents: From Interest to Impact: A Better Buying Funnel
description:
The rise of AI has changed how buyers interact with brands. Prospects are now coming to the table with more context than ever before. A single AI search can shape how they evaluate options long before a first conversation. 
The path from initial interest to signed deal looks different now. Outbound, inbound, nurture, automation, and sales execution are still part of the equation. What’s changing is how those pieces work together as buyers move through the funnel in less predictable ways. 
Join us on March 10 at 12 pm ET for a virtual panel discussion with experts across the full buying funnel who will share how teams are adapting their go-to-market approach as buyer behavior evolves. 
The 60-minute Business Growth Roundtable will cover:
Outbound sales and marketing approaches that earn attention and garner engagement
Inbound strategies that attract high-intent buyers
Lead nurturing tactics that turn interest into sales conversations
Practical ways teams are using AI to scale execution while preserving human judgment
Sales enablement approaches that shorten sales cycles and increase close rates 
Who Should Attend

title: Lunch With the Rabbi
description:
Join us for our monthly “Lunch with the Rabbi” class where each year a group of dedicated congregants meet and discuss a provocative Jewish book. This year we will be reading Rabbi Katz’s own Yochanan’s Gamble: Judaism’s Pragmatic Approach to Life. The book makes the claim that rather than have the intractable, strident discourse of today’s politics, we need to embrace the pragmatism of the Rabbis, which began with Yochanan ben Zakkai’s decision to trade Jerusalem of Yavneh. In essence, we can benefit today from the model of the ancient rabbis who make messy compromises, lead (but not too far ahead of their flock), change laws if they don’t work, and preserve peace within their community in order to better function in our day of division. 
Going forward, the class will meet monthly on the 2nd Tuesday of each month. Brown bag lunch (dairy or pareve) at 12pm, hybrid learning at 12:30pm. 
REGISTER HERE!

title: Parent Academy Series School Year 2025-2026
description:
The Parent Academy is an engaging monthly program that runs September through June where parents and caregivers can learn practical tools and strategies to support their youth’s mental well-being. Each session focuses on a specific mental health topic and provides helpful resources and tips that can be used right away. 
These sessions are psychoeducational webinars, not to be used in place of clinical treatment. 
TOPICS: 
Suicide Prevention – Wednesday 9/17 12-1:00pm
Developed by the American Foundation for Suicide Prevention, the session is designed to help parents, teachers, and other caring adults recognize the warning signs of suicide in youth, initiate conversations around depression and suicidal thoughts, and guide them in helping young people access needed support and resources.  
Teen Dating Violence: Visible & Voiced – Tuesday 10/21 6:30-7:30pm
This session equips parents with the tools to recognize signs of dating violence in teens and respond with confidence. Learn how to spot red flags, support open communication, and help your teen build safe, healthy relationships.  
Less Stress, More Joy: Holiday Prep for Parents – Wednesday 11/12 12-1:00pm
This interactive webinar will help you plan for a more peaceful and meaningful holiday season. NJ4S will share simple strategies and practical tools to help you set healthy boundaries, manage stress, and prioritize your well-being.  
Spotting the Signs: A Guide to Youth Substance Use Awareness – Tuesday 12/9 12-1:00pm
PRESENTED BY Center for Prevention and Counseling
Participants will be equipped with tools and knowledge to recognize and respond to youth substance use.  
Suicide & Social Media – Wednesday 1/21 6:30-8:00pm
PRESENTED BY Jonathan Singer Ph.D., LCSW
This webinar will translate the most important research on social media and suicide risk into practical tips.  
Eating Disorders 101 – Tuesday 2/24 6:30-7:30pm
Information, signs to recognize, and resources will be shared on the topics of anorexia nervosa, bulimia nervosa, and binge-eating disorders.  
High Stakes: The Rising Risk of Youth Gambling – Tuesday 3/10 12-1:00pm
PRESENTED BY New Bridge Services Information will be shared about the growing issue of youth gambling, shedding light on trends, what to look for, and available resources.  
Parent/Caregiver Support for Children on the Spectrum – Tuesday 4/21 6:30-7:30pm 
The session will provide a safe, supportive, and empowering space for parents/caregivers of children on the autism spectrum to connect, share topics, and build resilience through tools for mental health, self-care, and caregiving.  
A Parent’s Guide to Youth Mental Health – Wednesday 5/13 6:30-7:30pm
This webinar will focus on the various aspects of mental health, how to have the conversation with a youth in your life, and what to expect after the conversation.  
Bored to Brave: Building Independence through Community Adventures this Summer – Tuesday 6/2 12-1:00pm
This webinar will cover the impact of changing schedules, empowering youth to reduce screen time, and creating opportunities within the home, neighborhood, and community. Our NJ4S summer programs will be noted, as well as free resources within the counties.

During Daytime Bible Study, we discuss the Bible and books that analyze the Bible in depth. We also have fun and food. In 2024, we studied Mark and screened films Sabbath and 1946: The Mistranslation That Shifted A Culture. We read books about Holy Week, the Sabbath, The Sermon On The Mount, and Biblical women. All reference resources, translations, and interpretations are welcome. 
Current Book: “Who Stole My Bible? Reclaiming Scripture As A Handbook For Resisting Tyranny” by Rev. Jennifer Butler. 
Past Bible Study Books Have Included:
Sabbath As Resistance by Walter Brueggeman
Entering The Passion Of Jesus by Amy-Jill Levine
Jesus’ Alternative Plan: The Sermon On The Mount by Richard Rohr

title: Practical Spanish 2
location: Montclair Main - Classroom, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

In this class you will continue to build on the knowledge acquired in Practical Spanish 1 or Spanish 1A. You will expand your vocabulary and continue to practice and add to your repertoire of useful expressions.

Topics covered:


	Additional conversation/questions- ordering food/ shopping/making a reservation/airport/train station; asking and talk about likes and dislikes;  locating buildings or monuments
	Making polite requests
	Interrogative adjectives- Qué/Cómo/Cuándo/Dónde/Quién/Por qué
	Forming questions
	More ar/ir/er regular and irregular verbs
	Gustar/IOP
	Ask and tell time
	Possessive adjectives and different ways of showing possession (His book/John’s book)
	Weather
	Idiomatic expressions with Tener
	Contrast and apply the use of Ser/ Estar.
	Culture/Food/Holidays/Traditions
	All related vocabulary



Prerequisite: Completion of Practical Spanish 1, Spanish 1A or equivalent.

title: Writing: Method and Motivation
location: Montclair Main - Conference Room, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

A workshop for anyone who wants to write, or wants to infuse their existing writing practice with new energy. In each class, we'll consider one element of fiction or creative non-fiction, such as point of view, narrative voice, and what makes a piece of writing riveting, or boring. Students can expect in-class writing prompts, reading aloud, and optional homework. Positive encouragement, rather than formal critiques,  will be featured, and inspiration and organization for writers will be discussed. Students should bring whatever they use to write to each class.

This course will cover:
- Advanced use of saws, drills, routers, kreg jigs, dowel jigs, biscuit jointers, table saw, router table
- Various types of joinery, fasteners, and finishing
- Different edge finishes on router table
- Carving tools
- Corner clamps, bar clamps

PREREQUISITE: Completion of Woodworking 101.

Materials fee is dependent on project and payable to instructor at the beginning of class.
Small Shelves / Spice Rack / Record Storage Box: $20-$25
Shelves / End Tables / Night Stands / Stools: $60-75
Coffee Table: $100

title: NASO General Body Meeting
description:
Join us for the Native African Student Organization's General Body Meeting! This is a space where we come together to celebrate and explore the richness of African cultures, while also connecting with other cultures from around the world. At each meeting, we'll engage in lively discussions, learn about Africa’s diverse history, traditions, and current events, and empower each other to understand and embrace our heritage. Whether you're looking to deepen your cultural knowledge, make meaningful connections, or simply have fun with fellow students who share a passion for Africa and global cultures, this is the place to be! Let’s build a supportive community, amplify African voices on campus, and create lasting friendships. All are welcome, come, learn, share, and connect!

Hosted by: Native African Student Organization

title: Tales on the Trails - Seed Starting
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Tales on the Trails 
Wednesdays, 1:00 PM -1:45 PM 
Age: Preschool 3-5 year olds 
March 11th – Seed Starting 
Spring is right around the corner, which means it’s time to start thinking about what to plant in your garden.  
Discover the wonders of the outdoor world with your little one.  This program begins in the gardens of Van Vleck with a story, then continues with a walk and concludes with a simple craft or activity.  All programs focus on the theme listed.  A parent or caregiver must accompany each child.  Programs will be held outdoors, weather permitting. 
Sponsored by The Albert Payson Terhune Foundation  
Fee: $10/class per child for Members / $15/class per child for General Public 
Registration required by 1 day prior to each class 
 
  
Upcoming Classes 
April 8th – Beauty of Trees 
April’s Tales on the Trails class is all about trees. Learn about trees, their leaves, and the important role they play in our gardens. 
  
May 27th – Sounds of Spring 
Do you hear the birds singing, the wind blowing, and rabbits scurrying around? These are all the sounds of spring. Join us for Tales on the Trails as we use our senses to explore the wonders of spring. 
  
June 17th – Magic of Moths 
What’s flying around the gardens? Is it a butterfly or could it be a moth? Learn about moths at this month’s Tales on the Trails.

title: Database Research Strategies and Advanced Search Tips
description:
In this workshop, learn some practical ways to increase the effectiveness of your database searches. As databases have become larger, searching has actually become more difficult to do well without knowing more about how these databases are being developed and managed today. We’ll discuss using Boolean searching tools to manipulate search results, how to engage with database thesauri and a controlled vocabulary, linked subject headings, setting up search alerts, and more.
Register Here: https://montclair.libcal.com/event/16500099

Join contemporary spiritual teacher and author Ramananda John E. Welshons (One Soul, One Love, One Heart, When Prayers Aren’t Answered and Awakening From Grief) for a series of evening meditation classes in the comfort of your own home via Zoom. These classes will focus on specific practices for working with fear and anxiety.  Participants will learn and practice different forms of mindfulness meditation in each session and will have the opportunity to participate in discussions of Ramananda’s three critically acclaimed books. title: Lenten Study "The Tears of Things - Wednesday evenings March 11,18,25 @7:30pm on Zoom
description:
How do we live compassionately in a time of violence and despair? What can we do with our private disappointments and the anger that we feel in such an unjust world? These are the questions many of us carry on our hearts.  This Lent, we will gather to explore them with Franciscan friar,Richard Rohr, as he turns to the writings of the Hebrew prophets to offer us a path forward today.

The prophets' writings reflect the full spectrum of human maturity.  In almost every case their criticism of culture and institutions take them on a journey from anger to sadness and ultimately, to compassion. They confront evil and injustice in a way that acknowledges the wholeness of history, the interconnectedness of every living being, and the reality of divine and universal love.  In doing so, they set the stage for Jesus, who follows this identical pattern. 

This Lent, join Rev. Ann for three Zoom sessions to explore what this ancient wisdom has to teach us in our own time of outrage and heartbreak. We encourage you to read the book to prepare for this study.  You can order it online HERE or through your favorite bookstore. 

To join the class click the ZOOM LINK HERE

We look forward to taking this Lenten journey together.

title: Let's Listen To...Alto Sax: Beyond B-Bop (MPL Auditorium)
description: Curator and renowned saxophonist/composer/educator Rudresh Mahanthappa guides us through the music of alto players who innovated and set the pace in the wake of Charlie Parker, Jackie McLean, Eric Dolphy, Steve Coleman, and Gary Bartz, to name a few. A longtime Montclair resident, Rupresh is one of the leading voices pushing jazz forward. He has won best altoist in the famed DownBeat Critics Poll an astounding nine times. His records consistently receive critical praise. Since 2019, Rupresh has been director of jazz at Princeton University.

title: Author Chat - "Give Her Credit" - With Grace Williams NEW
location: Montclair Main - Conference Room, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a free Adult School class. Registration is required. Click here to register.

Did you know that women in the mid-1970s still could not get a credit card, a mortgage or a business loan without a male co-signer? Have you ever wondered just how this sorry state of affairs came to change?  Join us for a conversation between Grace Williams, financial reporter, podcast co-host, and author of "Give Her Credit: The Untold Account of a Women's Bank That Empowered a Generation," and Eleanor Galt Lin, a certified financial planner and advisory at Merrill Lynch, to learn more about a remarkable group of women in Denver who disrupted the 1970s male-run world of business, banking, and finance--and made history.

Do You have a huge Pokémon fan? Is your child starting to ask about manga and anime? Maybe you’re just curious about the hype around manga and anime.

Well this workshop is for you!

Join us as we explore the different genres within anime/manga, how manga is beneficial to readers, and how to navigate the growing landscape that is manga!

Parents will get to take home sheets listing several manga terms and a book list of some of the manga we have in our collection.

title: Inspiration trap creativity workshop
description:
Live African music and drumming events in NJ
6,7&8pm Issyra Gallery presents - Inspiration Trap Creativity workshop

Only 10 attendees allowed social distancing and mandatory mask wearing.

You are invited to participate in a special session of creative thinking and fresh insight dedicated to inspiration.

We live in a world of constant change that requires us to be able to continually adapt and innovate in order to succeed. Unfortunately, many of us have forgotten how to delve into our raw creativity and leave behind the rigidity of the frameworks we have progressively interiorized.
This unique workshop seeks to reclaim our inspiration and imagination, rethink the unthinkable. It is ideal for people working in the creative arts as well as students, entrepreneurs, team managers, corporate leaders and anyone looking to implement their creative vision.

Inspiration is a vital driving force in our lives that moves us to create our best work and lights the way for new, authentic ideas. How do we invite, cultivate and harness inspiration so that we are able to bring these ideas into reality?
As a self-taught artist exploring various avenues of self-expression, Issa Sow draws a distinction between the phenomenon of inspiration itself and our task of making ourselves ready for it.
In the upcoming Inspiration Trap workshop, we will imagine ways we can invite and contain our inspiration, and well as keep our mind in the open, dynamic state as a receptacle or a “canvas” to collect, reflect and grow our ideas.
Feel free to bring along a personal object, a piece of clothing or even a small piece of furniture to play with during this hands-on experiment in innovative thinking. There are surprises and new elements in each session!

Issa =2
Issa Sow is a contemporary international autodidact artist known as Issa=2.
He believes in life being a journey of continual growth with each new

description:

Friday, March 13 
11:30 AM – 1:00 PM
12:00 PM – 1:30 PM
1:00 PM – 2:30 PM
2:30 PM – 4:00 PM
Join us for an enchanting Cream Tea in the Library at Van Vleck House! 
Upon arrival, you will be offered a menu of fine teas and infusions. Your choice of tea will be brewed to perfection. Once everyone is settled in, the tiers will arrive with freshly baked scones, jam, homemade clotted cream, scrumptious sandwiches, and a dessert. We hope you will join us! 
This event is for adults and children ages 13+. For group bookings of more than 8 guests, please contact richard@secrettearoom.us. Seatings are 90 minutes, and registration is required. 
Dietary Restrictions
A gluten-free option is available with prior notice. We are not able to accommodate a dairy-free or vegan diet. All requests must be communicated to us 48 hours before the event. You may note this request on the checkout page. For dietary/allergy inquiries, please contact richard@secrettearoom.us. 
Cancellation Policy
We are unable to accommodate cancellations or rescheduling. If you find you are no longer able to attend your scheduled event, you may transfer your ticket(s) to another person(s) to attend instead. Please notify Van Vleck House and Gardens so we can update your ticket name(s) for event check-in. 
Please note that this event is a fundraiser, and a portion of your ticket price will go towards supporting our beloved Van Vleck House & Gardens. 
Member Price: $45
General Price: $55 
A 10% gratuity for your server is included in tea pricing.

title: Be Internet Awesome: Internet Safety For Kids (2nd-5th Graders)
location: Montclair Main - Storytime Room, Montclair Public Library
description:
In today's digital landscape, children need more than just access -- they need guidance, values, and tools to navigate it responsibly. That's where Be Internet Awesome comes in! In this program series, they'll learn the five Pillars: Smart, Alert, Strong, Kind, and Brave. These Pillars teach kids to think critically, protect their privacy, stand up to cyberbullying, and build a culture of respect and empathy online. 

Parents are encouraged to join our sessions. We will have some take-home materials available after each session to continue the learning at home! 

This session will cover the pillar: KIND

What the kids will learn: Kids explore how empathy helps them get along with others online and how they can be upstanders when they see mean behavior.

title: Rail, Rail, The Gang's All Here
description:
Metrotrails: NJ, PA, NY Metropolitan Area Hiking and More!
"The West Essex Trail is a former railroad track bed of the Erie Lackawanna Railroad Caldwell Branch now hiking trail located mostly in Essex County, New Jersey in the United States, with a small section in Passaic County, New Jersey."
We will be following the remainder of the branch that was sadly not converted to trail from the Passaic River as far as we can north to Bloomfield Ave, Verona where the current trail starts then make our way back through parks and trails.

Other points of interest: the gorgeous Grover Cleveland Park, the Trotter Tract, some of the Lenape Trail and some local parks. Route is subject to change.

Total mileage should be under 10. There will be several opportunities for food in both directions. This is a loop hike.

Route is approximately this:
https://www.google.com/maps/d/edit?mid=1VxPj7usL7tQ2D3Txbhstw3ZjCBklbeE&usp=sharing

title: Yoga @ the Library (Tweens & Grownups)
location: Montclair Main - Auditorium, Montclair Public Library
description:
Wrap up the week with a refreshing yoga session for tweens and their grownups! (No unaccompanied grownups, please.) Instructor Brittany Martorella will lead you through gentle yoga poses and breathing exercises to help achieve inner peace. Registration required.

Participants should wear socks and bring their own bath/beach towels or yoga mats.

Meet your instructor:
Brittany Martorella is a seasoned yoga teacher with over a decade of experience honing her craft. Originally certified by Yoga to the People in NYC, Brittany's journey has led her to share her expertise internationally, from the bustling streets of NYC and Brooklyn to the serene landscapes of Costa Rica, where she studied and taught at Peace Retreat. Specializing in Vinyasa, Brittany's classes offer a dynamic blend of movement and breath, invigorating the body and soothing the soul. Her love for restorative postures provides students with a chance to deeply relax and rejuvenate.
With her infectious laughter, she loves to remind her students that yoga is meant to be fun. She creates an atmosphere of light-heartedness and levity, setting the stage for true calm and zen focus in every class. Join Brittany on the mat and let's laugh, flow, and grow together!

title: Erev Shabbat Service
description:
Erev Shabbat services begin on Friday at 6:30pm. These services are especially musical, often with a band of congregants accompanying the Cantor. Usually around an hour, these services are filled with a lot of spirit. It’s not uncommon to see people dancing in their seat or hear the whole congregation clapping their hands. Friday night is intergenerational. Glance around and see senior citizens sitting beside religious school families both getting something out of the services.  
In the summer months, services are led by members of the congregation with support from our clergy.
Join us most weeks from anywhere for Erev Shabbat Services through Zoom or our YouTube Livestream.

title: Qigong At FCC! March 14, 12Noon in the Guild Room
description:
Come and practice some Qigong at FCC. Part of the Taoist tradition, this exercise system uses very slow, deliberate movement, keeping your mind actively involved. The style of movement helps reduce physical and mental tension to help us remain grounded and centered.

Class is led by FCC member, Joanne Kornoelje, who has been practicing Tai Chi and Qigong for over 30 years. She has taught all levels from children to seniors, and it remains a daily practice in her life.  Come and give it a try!

title: Creative Liturgy Workshop
description:
The prayers we recite by rote each week were not handed down fully formed from heaven—they were written by real people, reaching for words to express their hopes and fears, their loves and losses. Over time, those personal prayers became the shared liturgy of the Jewish people. But sometimes, the ancient words of others don’t quite resonate, which is why the process of prayer writing must be ongoing. Join Rabbi James Feder once a month to explore new liturgy being written for our current moment and take a hand at writing some yourself. 
Dates: September 27, October 11, November 22, December 13, January 24, February 7, March 14, April 25, May 30

title: Inner Body Meditation
description:
The Mindful Stream
"Awareness of the inner body takes you beyond the mind." - Eckhart Tolle

In this mediation session we will bring our attention here: to the inner body. Sessions are suitable for newcomers, those experienced in Tolle's teachings and anyone wishing to remain completely anonymous. They will last around 20 minutes and cameras and microphones will be disabled.

(Depending on the situation, a free discussion may be held at the end with mics and cameras allowed.)

See you there!

Please be aware that sessions will close if no members have joined after 10 minutes.

Emrys also organises a free Work of Byron Katie online mediation group. Visit [this Meetup Group link](https://www.meetup.com/thework-runsfree/) for more information.

title: Women in the Struggle: Historic Stamps with Clarence McKnight (All ages welcome)
description:
Families are invited to learn about commemorative stamps featuring women throughout history as presented by expert philatelist Clarence McKnight. Learn about their contributions to the Civil Rights movement, and suffrage and equality, and social justice. In addition to examples from his collection, Mr. McKnight will present a PowerPoint about the stamps and the social justice champions who inspired them, and will be followed by guest speakers from the Montclair Chapter of NCNW (National Council of Negro Women, and young poets. 

Don’t miss this chance to see Mr. McKnight’s singular collection up close! There will be refreshments and snacks. Made possible by funds from the New Jersey State Council on the Arts, a partner agency of the National Endowment for the Arts. Registration recommended for updates and reminders.

“Come Celebrate With Me” is a year-long series of programming honoring 100 years of observing Black history in America. Founded in 1926 by Carter G. Woodson, Negro History Week evolved into Black History Month, opening the door for additional annual commemorations such as Juneteenth, Black Music Month, Caribbean Heritage Month, Kwanzaa and more.

Montclair Public Library will honor the centennial of these commemorations in 2026 through a series of programs designed to engage and connect participants while highlighting and celebrating the experiences and contributions of people of African descent in the United States.

 

This exhibit is from the private collection of Clarence McKnight.
I’ve been collecting stamps since 1986. Over the years, I have presented at churches in
Essex and Bergen County. I also presented to youth groups such as, The Links, Jack
and Jill in Montclair and Englewood, New Jersey, Scouting groups in Montclair and
elementary schools in Northern New Jersey, New York and most recently in Maryland
and DC. Some of my presentations included Power Point and videos presentations. I’ve
also setup exhibits and gave presentations for Prudential Insurance in Hanover in New
Jersey, Hoffman Roche in Nutley, New Jersey, Municipality of East Orange and
Municipality of Perth Amboy, Perth Amboy Art Gallery, Newark Museum. Yogi Berra
Museum on Montclair University Campus, New Jersey, local libraries (Montclair,
Edison, Perth Amboy, East Orange and Newark), New York National Stamp show,
Rutgers University in Newark, Kean University, Passaic County Community College in
Wanaque and Passaic campus, Housing Authority of Passaic ( Senior Citizen Building)
Spree Village After School Program in Passaic, plus Post Offices in Hudson, Bergen,
Essex and Passaic Counties, Somerset Patriots Minor league baseball team for Negro
Baseball night. Throughout the years my exhibits are during African American History
Month, Hispanic Heritage Month, Women’s History Month and Negro League Baseball.
I’m the former New Jersey State Director, editor of Reflections newsletter and board
member of The Ebony Society of Philatelic Events and Reflections (ESPER), an
international stamp club promoting the collecting of African-Americans on stamps. I’m
the webmaster for the award-winning ESPER website. For more information visit:
ESPERSTAMPS.ORG Contact information: clarencmac@aol.com

title: NJ Code & Coffee meetup - March
description:
NJ Code & Coffee
Calling all New Jersey techies to the **NJ Code and Coffee** tech community meetup at the EqualSpace ES550 Tech & Innovation campus!

Code and Coffee is an inclusive, informal networking/co-working session for those in tech (and the tech-curious).
That includes operators, founders, and investors. Developers, data engineers, UX/UI designers, data scientists, product managers, software engineers, solution architects... **all are welcome!**

People of all skill and experience levels can attend, we love it that way. Many people (optionally) bring projects to work on, and many other people (optionally) socialize the entire time. It's entirely up to you!

Come grab a cup of coffee and meet new people, share stories, find collaborators, talk about everything tech, talk about everything NOT tech, take a break from job applications, get away from the computer screen, make connections, make conversation, **make friends!**

**Agenda:**
**2pm:** Doors open, networking starts
**2:30pm:** Intro circle
**3pm:** networking continues
**5pm:** event ends

**The Intro Circle**
**Near the beginning of the event (about 2:30 pm), we will do a standup in a circle:**
We first talk about organizer announcements, updates, and logistics. Then we go around the circle where each person says:

* your name and job title/ role in tech/ tech stack,
* What you can help others with,
* What you would like help with,
* Job opportunities you're hiring for OR announce that you are looking for one. If none, that's cool.

After the introduction circle, everything is self-organized.

**Community Discord - come hang out before and after!:**
[www.codeandcoffee.chat](www.codeandcoffee.chat)

**The Venue**
Equal Space is a premium share-space created with multicultural business owners in mind, =SPACE equips founders with resources, programming and a powerful network.

Headquartered in the City of Newark, their share-space provides resources for startups, coaching for tech companies, and a wealth of intellectuals to collaborate and build with. The next wave in enterprises is being born within their walls. Become a part of the story: [EqualSpace.co/](https://equalspace.co/)

title: St. Patty's Day Bar Crawl - North Jersey
description:
North Jersey Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis And More (BVT)
Come join the group for a St. Patty's Day Bar Crawl in Hoboken.

We'll be starting at The Shepherd & the Knucklehead of Hoboken, 1313 Willow Ave., at 6:30 p.m. and then walking around from there.

(tentative schedule below)

Of course, join us at any time. We'll try to post updates in the comments as the night unfolds. Feel free to call or text Andy Milone at 201-234-9609, or direct message him on Meetup or on Whatsapp, if you can't find us or have questions.

It's totally optional to wear your favorite St. Patrick's Day attire and/or consume a few adult beverages. Feel free to just come and have a good time hanging with familiar faces and meeting new ones.

**6:30 p.m.**

* The Shepherd & the Knucklehead of Hoboken - 1313 Willow Ave.

**8:30 p.m.**

* The Ale House - 1034 Willow Ave.

**9:30 p.m.**

* 10th & Willow Bar & Grill - 935 Willow Ave.

**11:30 p.m.**

* Finnegan's Pub - 734 Willow Ave.

**12 a.m.**

* Farside Tavern - 531 Washington St.
* Louise & Jerry's - 329 Washington St. (Optional)
* Black Bear Hoboken Bar & Grill - 205 Washington St. (Optional)
* Mike's Wild Moose Saloon – 92 River St. (Optional)

Please be smart about travel. The train station is not far away and runs late. Parking can be a challenge, but there is street parking (where residential permit is not required, and several paid public parking garages, including at 112 River St. and 1330 Willow Ave. Feel free to comment below if interested in carpooling.

This event follows our Halloween and Holiday bar crawls, a trend spurred by Ray and Raymond, two regular attendees of our events.

Questions? Suggestions? Concerns? You can reach out to Andy Milone at 201-234-9609 (call/text), or by message over Meetup or Whatsapp.

title: Forest Regeneration Volunteer Workday
description:
South Mountain Conservancy Activities Meetup Group
Forest Regeneration activities focus on the areas in and around the fenced 14-acre Forest and Wildflower Preserve located near the Dog Park on Bear Lane in the Reservation. On workdays, we focus on removing invasive plants like knotweed, garlic mustard and mugwort. Ages 12 to 15 are welcome with a parent or guardian present. Ages 15 and above require a signature by a parent or guardian on a liability waiver; teenagers cannot just be dropped off.

Please arrive 10 to 15 minutes early so we can hike into the Wildflower Preserve at 9 am sharp.

Please dress appropriately! There is some poison ivy on trails and in work areas. Wear long pants, long-sleeved shirt, closed-toe shoes or rain boots, bug spray, and layer as needed. We supply tools. If you have gloves, bring them or you can borrow a pair from us. Bring your own water.

For information, contact program co-coordinators Rich Rockwell and Tom Pollard at forestregen@[somocon.org](http://somocon.org/)

To see what's blooming in the Wildflower Preserve, check out our [Wildflower Preserve](https://www.inaturalist.org/projects/south-mountain-conservancy-wildflower-preserve) project on iNaturalist project.

**Meet at the tool sheds at the Dog Park at 11 Bear Lane, Maplewood NJ.**

title: Lenape Way Walks with Creation Justice Team (Offsite)
description: As part of our Lenten journey, we will hike some familiar and lesser-known parts of the Lenape Trail. It is not as old, long, well-known or as crowded as the Camino de Santiago in Spain, but parts of the trail are in Montclair and Bloomfield: Mills Reservation, the Iris Gardens, Tuers Park, Brookdale Park and Clark’s Pond. Join us on our walk together as we connect to ourselves, each other, and to the Lenape people indigenous to this land.

title: Pride Choir Rehearsal (Sanctuary and Assembly Room)
Second Sunday Series Concert: Youn-hee Kim
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Youn-hee Kim, returning to Van Vleck as a much-admired pianist, is on the piano faculty at both
Montclair State University’s John J. Cali School of Music and the university’s Extension Division. 
Born in Seoul, South Korea, and immigrating to the U.S. in 1998, Dr. Kim has garnered numerous accolades, including first prize at the 2000 Los Angeles International Liszt Competition, third prize at the Carmel International Piano Competition, and two-time grand prizes in the Music Associates Performance Award Competition in California. She was also the inaugural recipient of the Alicia de Larrocha Scholarship.In South Korea, she won first prize in the National Music Competition and third prize in the Samik Concours. Her performances have taken her to international stages, both as a soloist and with orchestras such as the New York Symphony Orchestra, the International Chamber Ensemble of Roma, L’Orchestra di Perugia in Italy, the Muenster Orchestra in Germany, and the Gangnam Symphony and Incheon Symphony Orchestra in South Korea. 
Dr. Kim holds degrees from California State University Fullerton, the Manhattan School of Music, and Stony Brook University (SUNY), where she studied on full-tuition scholarships. 
Register are strongly suggested at the link below. Seats are usually available just before the start of the concert when registrants do not attend or cancel their reservations. 
 
The concert is free but a donation of $10 is suggested to support the Second Sunday Series.

description:

Photographer’s Eye is a relaxed, beginner-friendly photography course designed for adults who want to learn how to see and take better pictures—no technical background required. Over two 3-hour sessions (6 hours total), we’ll explore the basics of visual storytelling, composition, light, and how photographers make creative choices. Through simple exercises, discussion, and image examples, participants will develop confidence and a stronger “photographic eye.” Any camera is welcome, including smartphones, point-and-shoots, or DSLRs.
