title: CALI SCHOOL OF MUSIC | Billy Hart | Jazz Drum Faculty Masterclass
description:
Don’t miss this rare opportunity to learn from one of the most influential voices in modern jazz drumming.
Legendary jazz drummer Billy Hart, known for his work with Herbie Hancock, Stan Getz, and countless others, leads a dynamic masterclass at the Cali School of Music.
Billy Hart is one of the most influential jazz drummers of his generation, celebrated for his versatility across straight-ahead, avant-garde, and popular styles. He has released 12 albums as a leader and appeared on more than 600 recordings, working with legends such as Herbie Hancock, Miles Davis, Wes Montgomery, Stan Getz, and Charles Lloyd. A founding member of Quest and a longtime member of the Cookers, Hart also leads his own acclaimed quartet. Born in Washington, D.C., he began performing as a teenager with Buck Hill and Shirley Horn before touring in the 1960s with Jimmy Smith, Wes Montgomery, and Eddie Harris. Since then, his career has spanned collaborations with both jazz icons and experimental innovators.

Hosted by: The College of the Arts

title: MILL @ MPL: Taste of Tai Chi
description:
Note: This is a class run through the MILL. Please register through their website here.

T'ai Chi Ch'uan is a 1000 year old art of mind-body health and energy cultivation. “A Taste of Tai Chi” introduces students to the 12-posture Yang-style Short Form of Master Bow Sim Mark and is designed for students of all ages and levels of physical ability. No prior experience with T'ai Chi is necessary. The health benefits of T'ai Chi include balance, flexibility, strength, immune-system support, and stress relief.

LEWIS FREEMAN studied the “Taste of T’ai Chi” form with Don Madison (Dragon T'ai Chi Ch'uan Association) and achieved the level of Assistant Teacher in July 2019. He previously studied the Yang-style short form at Long River T’aiChi Circle in Manhattan in the 1990s. When he's not practicing T’ai Chi, Lewis teaches at Fordham University as Senior Lecturer in Communication and Media Studies and also consults for businesses and organizations.

title: Montclair Garden Club Talk: Introduction to Native Gardening
location: Montclair Bellevue Ave Branch - Classroom, Montclair Bellevue Ave Branch - First Floor, Montclair Bellevue Avenue Branch
description:
Introduction to Native Gardening: The importance and benefits of native plants and how using native plants in home gardens provides the environment with connected habitats for conservation. Comprehensive review of New Jersey native plant species including trees, shrubs and perennials and where to source them.

Lecture presented by Essex County Master Gardener, Karen Fricke:

Born on a farm in central Illinois, Karen Fricke was digging in the dirt before she could walk.
Through her years as a magazine editor, high school teacher, graduate student, stay-at-home
mom to four, and fiber artist, she always found her way outside to the garden. She became a
Master Gardener in Maryland in 2014, and worked at a plant nursery, where she developed and
ran their educational workshop program. Karen and her husband moved to New Jersey in 2022
to be closer to children and grandchildren, and she transferred to the Essex County Master
Gardeners program, where she lectures for their Speakers Program on the importance of
incorporating Native Plants in our home gardens.

title: Practical Spanish 2
location: Montclair Main - Classroom, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

In this class you will continue to build on the knowledge acquired in Practical Spanish 1 or Spanish 1A. You will expand your vocabulary and continue to practice and add to your repertoire of useful expressions.

Topics covered:


	Additional conversation/questions- ordering food/ shopping/making a reservation/airport/train station; asking and talk about likes and dislikes;  locating buildings or monuments
	Making polite requests
	Interrogative adjectives- Qué/Cómo/Cuándo/Dónde/Quién/Por qué
	Forming questions
	More ar/ir/er regular and irregular verbs
	Gustar/IOP
	Ask and tell time
	Possessive adjectives and different ways of showing possession (His book/John’s book)
	Weather
	Idiomatic expressions with Tener
	Contrast and apply the use of Ser/ Estar.
	Culture/Food/Holidays/Traditions
	All related vocabulary



Prerequisite: Completion of Practical Spanish 1, Spanish 1A or equivalent.

title: Writing: Method and Motivation
location: Montclair Main - Conference Room, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

A workshop for anyone who wants to write, or wants to infuse their existing writing practice with new energy. In each class, we'll consider one element of fiction or creative non-fiction, such as point of view, narrative voice, and what makes a piece of writing riveting, or boring. Students can expect in-class writing prompts, reading aloud, and optional homework. Positive encouragement, rather than formal critiques,  will be featured, and inspiration and organization for writers will be discussed. Students should bring whatever they use to write to each class.

title: Advanced Woodworking
location: Montclair - Montclair High School, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

For those with previous woodworking experience, this class will allow students to learn more advanced techniques. Students will have the opportunity to start a new project or bring in a project from home that they would like to work on.

This course will cover:
- Advanced use of saws, drills, routers, kreg jigs, dowel jigs, biscuit jointers, table saw, router table
- Various types of joinery, fasteners, and finishing
- Different edge finishes on router table
- Carving tools
- Corner clamps, bar clamps

PREREQUISITE: Completion of Woodworking 101.

Materials fee is dependent on project and payable to instructor at the beginning of class.
Small Shelves / Spice Rack / Record Storage Box: $20-$25
Shelves / End Tables / Night Stands / Stools: $60-75
Coffee Table: $100

Emrys has been passionate about mindfulness since 2010 and since 2021 he has been organising free, weekly online meditation groups. For more information on his free Work of Byron Katie online mediation group, visit [this Meetup Group link](https://www.meetup.com/thework-runsfree/).

title: The DJ Academy: Music Matters 101
description:
DJing is one of the original elements of Hip Hop and is often considered the first. Hip Hop began in the Bronx during the 1970s at community block parties, where DJs used turntables to bring people together through music. Early pioneers like DJ Kool Herc introduced breakbeats by looping the most rhythmic parts of records, creating the foundation for breaking and shaping how dancers and MCs interacted with the music.
As Hip Hop evolved, DJing became the musical backbone of the culture. DJs provide the beats for MCing, set the tempo for dancers, and control the energy of Hip Hop spaces by reading the crowd and responding in real time. Through techniques like scratching, cutting, and beat juggling, DJs helped define Hip Hop’s sound while preserving its history and passing down cultural knowledge.
This class introduces the basics of DJing and how music shapes the dance experience. DJ Bascis will explain core concepts such as tempo, rhythm, and song structure, helping students understand how music guides movement and energy on the dance floor.
Participants will learn how DJs choose music, create smooth transitions, and adjust the vibe based on the room. 
Through simple examples and live demonstrations, students will build confidence in listening to music with intention. This can bewhether they are dancing, freestyling, or interested in DJing themselves.
This class is designed for beginners and college students, with no prior DJ or music experience required.

Hosted by: Campus Activities at Bloomfield

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12095719

description:

Through live demonstrations and guided practice, participants will learn to create custom GPTs that support authentic assessments, such as role-plays and case studies. The workshop will emphasize responsible and ethical AI use, highlighting how thoughtfully designed custom GPTs can enhance student engagement, support high-order thinking, and enable authentic, practice-based learning experiences. During these meetings, new members build connections with one another, develop leadership and personal growth skills, and gain a deeper understanding of what it means to be a lifelong member of the organization. NME meetings create a supportive environment where new members can ask questions, reflect on their experiences, and prepare for initiation while strengthening their sense of belonging within the chapter.

Hosted by: Sigma Sigma Sigma

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12229579

title: Chalice Choir Rehearsal
description:
The Chalice Choir is open to anyone who enjoys singing. No need to audition, and reading music is not a requirement. The choir sings a wide repertoire of music, from Handel to Nina Simone, Leonard Cohen and Bobby McFerrin. Opportunities to solo or sing in small groups are often available for more confident singers.

Regular rehearsals are on Wednesday evenings from 7:30 to 9:00 PM in the Sanctuary. For more information contact Markus.

title: Montclair Bee City Kick-Off
location: Montclair Fire House, 1 Pine St., Montclair, NJ, 07042
description:
Learn about Montclair’s new designation as a Bee City and how you can get involved!



Join us on March 18 at 7 pm at the Montclair Fire House, 1 Pine St., to learn how the NEEC, as Montclair’s official Bee City facilitator, will be working with the township and local organizations, including the Montclair Garden Club and the Native Plant Society of NJ, to make the town more bee- and pollinator-friendly. 
All are welcome to come, find out about local pollinator garden projects—from meadows to corner gardens to sidewalk planters—and explore simple ways to make your own space a haven for native bees, butterflies, and other pollinators. No space is too small! 
If your organization has an interest in local parks, ecological gardening, pollinators, or wildlife habitat and would like to take part in this initiative, please let us know. Contact Dave Wasmuth at MontclairBeeCityUSA@neearth.org. For more details about Bee City Montclair, visit our website. 
Register for the Bee City event.

title: Virtual Meditation
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Virtual Meditation 
Instructor: John Welshons 
Wednesdays, 7:00 PM – 9:00 PM 
January 7, 14, 21, 28 
February 4, 11, 25 
March 11, 18, 25 
April 1, 8, 15, 22, 29 
Join contemporary spiritual teacher and author Ramananda John E. Welshons (One Soul, One Love, One Heart, When Prayers Aren’t Answered and Awakening From Grief) for a series of evening meditation classes in the comfort of your own home via Zoom. These classes will focus on specific practices for working with fear and anxiety.  Participants will learn and practice different forms of mindfulness meditation in each session and will have the opportunity to participate in discussions of Ramananda’s three critically acclaimed books. Open to beginners and experienced practitioners, these gatherings offer an opportunity to experience deep inner peace while practicing with a highly experienced meditation teacher.  
Important Updated Registration Information: 
A Zoom Link will be sent the day of the class. If you are registering after 3 PM on the day of the class, the Zoom link will be included in the automated payment confirmation email sent when you register. There is no registration deadline for this class. 
Fee: Single class: $10 Members / $15 General Public

title: Safe Space Training
description:
Our Safe Space Training is an intensive, interactive training that provides attendees with information and tools to combat homophobia, biphobia, transphobia, cissexism and heterosexism, on campus and beyond. Through interactive activities and discussion, participants will personally reflect on their own experiences to enhance their personal and professional development, provide further education, and finally, identify ways to create change on campus and beyond.
All sessions are three hours and are first-come, first-served. Pre-registration is required. Registrants are expected to attend the full session. Only individuals who have fully completed the Safe Space Program and have agreed to the expectations of its membership are able to display the sticker/button of our program. Refreshments are not provided; however, you are more than welcome to bring your lunch, coffee, snacks, etc.

Hosted by: Advocacy and Outreach

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/11332855

description:
Step into the brand-new PLAE Room and discover upLIFT—a judgement-free program designed to help every student, from complete beginners to seasoned gym-goers, feel confident and capable with free-weight training. Learn proper lifting technique, get personalized form checks, and master the basics of creating your own effective workout programs. Whether you’re looking to meet other students who love to lift, pursue new fitness goals, or simply feel more at home in the weight room, upLIFT offers a supportive community and a dedicated space to grow. title: Spanish Writing Center - Workshop Series - Online - March 2026
description:
This workshop is designed for students who wish to deepen their understanding of the use of the past tense and imperfect tense in storytelling. Through clear explanations, contextualized examples, and practical activities, we will analyze the differences between completed actions and habitual or ongoing actions in the past. Participants will learn to identify when to use each tense to improve clarity, accuracy, and fluency in their written and oral storytelling.

description:
Lead Change in Child Advocacy and Policy When children and families face complex challenges, they need informed, compassionate professionals to stand up, speak out, and shape solutions. If you're ready to turn your commitment into action, we're here to help. We're hosting a virtual panel featuring alumni from our program on March 19 at 6:00 PM (EST). This is a no-pressure, exploratory space to hear directly from professionals who were once in your shoes and determine if our program fits your goals. This session will cover the following programs: 

Online Master of Arts in Child Advocacy and Policy
Child Advocacy (Certificate) – Online

Why Montclair? Completing the certificate program allows you to transfer all 15 credits towards the Master of Arts in Child Advocacy, in accordance with all related graduate policy.

title: Inspiration trap creativity workshop
description:
Live African music and drumming events in NJ
6,7&8pm Issyra Gallery presents - Inspiration Trap Creativity workshop

Only 10 attendees allowed social distancing and mandatory mask wearing.

You are invited to participate in a special session of creative thinking and fresh insight dedicated to inspiration.

We live in a world of constant change that requires us to be able to continually adapt and innovate in order to succeed. Unfortunately, many of us have forgotten how to delve into our raw creativity and leave behind the rigidity of the frameworks we have progressively interiorized.
This unique workshop seeks to reclaim our inspiration and imagination, rethink the unthinkable. It is ideal for people working in the creative arts as well as students, entrepreneurs, team managers, corporate leaders and anyone looking to implement their creative vision.

Inspiration is a vital driving force in our lives that moves us to create our best work and lights the way for new, authentic ideas. How do we invite, cultivate and harness inspiration so that we are able to bring these ideas into reality?
As a self-taught artist exploring various avenues of self-expression, Issa Sow draws a distinction between the phenomenon of inspiration itself and our task of making ourselves ready for it.
In the upcoming Inspiration Trap workshop, we will imagine ways we can invite and contain our inspiration, and well as keep our mind in the open, dynamic state as a receptacle or a “canvas” to collect, reflect and grow our ideas.
Feel free to bring along a personal object, a piece of clothing or even a small piece of furniture to play with during this hands-on experiment in innovative thinking. There are surprises and new elements in each session!

Issa =2
Issa Sow is a contemporary international autodidact artist known as Issa=2.
He believes in life being a journey of continual growth with each new

title: Eagle Rock Reservation
description:
Let's Walk!!
JOIN US FOR A WALK. WE WALK AT A MODERATE PACE ON UNPAVED PATHS.

We will hike for about an hour - 3.5 miles. We will be walking on trails in the woods, so appropriate footwear, long pants & socks, and bug spray.

Will will meet at:
[https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7](https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7) Eagle Rock Trail Parking Lot by Kiosk#1

If you enter from Eagle Rock Way, go pass the Highlawn and stay left. There will be a parking lot at the end of the curve to your right.

Eagle Rock Reservation is 408 acres of hills, streams, and valleys located on the borders of West Orange, Verona, and Montclair, New Jersey. Though located in the middle of the most densely populated county in the most densely populated state in the country, the Reservation is mostly undeveloped and boasts an extensive trail system within its borders.

**Please make sure to RSVP and arrive 5 minutes prior to start time.**

FOLLOW US ON INSTAGRAM - letswalkmeetup

Disclaimer: If you join us for a hike, please understand that you are responsible for your own preparedness and well-being and will hold no one else liable in case of injury or mishap. Event will be canceled in the event of extreme weather.

title: CALI SCHOOL OF MUSiC |  Masterclass with Kyle Resnick
description:
Masterclass with Kyle Resnick
Free and Open to the Public, Reservations not Required
Join the brass area for a very special masterclass with trumpeter Kyle Resnick. Kyle will work with Cali students and share what he's learned on his incredible musical journey that has seen him performing and recording with Taylor Swift, Ed Sheeran, Bruce Springsteen, Beirut, The National, and Mumford and Sons, among others!
Bio:
Kyle Resnick is a trumpet player known for his versatility and contributions to both live performances and studio recordings across a wide variety of genres and musical styles. He has established himself as a prominent figure in the music industry through collaborations with a diverse array of artists and groups, from recording for Taylor Swift (“Folklore,” “Evermore,” and “Midnights”), Ed Sheeran and Bruce Springsteen, among several others, to Classical chamber and solo and countless orchestral performances on concert stages over three decades. He has felt equally at home on stages like Carnegie Hall, Lincoln Center, the Barbican, Royal Albert Hall, Sydney Opera House and venues such as the Hollywood Bowl, and arenas and stadiums around the globe. Kyle is a longstanding touring and recording member of the bands The National and Beirut, and has commenced in 2025 on a world tour with the band Mumford & Sons, for which he has also recorded on the last two albums. Since his time as a student to the present day, Kyle has found great satisfaction enriching his musical journey through teaching trumpet and directing ensembles, having served on the faculties of Juilliard Pre-College, Brooklyn College, SUNY University at Buffalo and SUNY Buffalo State.

Hosted by: The College of the Arts

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12228722

title: Be Internet Awesome: Internet Safety For Kids (2nd-5th Graders)
location: Montclair Main - Storytime Room, Montclair Public Library
description:
In today's digital landscape, children need more than just access -- they need guidance, values, and tools to navigate it responsibly. That's where Be Internet Awesome comes in! In this program series, they'll learn the five Pillars: Smart, Alert, Strong, Kind, and Brave. These Pillars teach kids to think critically, protect their privacy, stand up to cyberbullying, and build a culture of respect and empathy online. 

Parents are encouraged to join our sessions. We will have some take-home materials available after each session to continue the learning at home! 

This session will cover the pillar: BRAVE

What the kids will learn: Kids consider who they can turn to when something online feels confusing or wrong. They're reminded that brave doesn't mean going it alone.

title: Worship Night (Campus Invasion)
location: Student Center 250BC - Ballrooms B and C
description:
Campus Invasion is a night set apart to lift up the name of Jesus at Montclair State University. On March 20th, we gather not just for music, but for worship—creating space for God’s presence to move freely among us.
This night is an invitation to pause from the busyness of campus life, lay down distractions, and come before God with open hearts. We believe worship is more than a song; it is surrender, alignment, and response to who God is. As we worship together, we are trusting God to refresh weary hearts, restore hope, and awaken a deeper hunger for Him.
Campus Invasion is about unity—students, friends, and the campus community coming together to seek God, pray, and declare Jesus as Lord over our lives and our campus. Whether you’ve been walking with God for years or you’re simply curious and searching, this night is for you.
Come expectant. Come ready to worship. Come as you are.Jesus will be glorified.

Hosted by: I lift Jesus

title: [Outside Org]  Montclair Alumnae Chapter-Delta Sigma Theta
Balsam Lake Mountain Hike
description:
Take in classic Catskill views on this scenic hike to Balsam Lake Mountain via the Dry Brook Ridge Trail in the Balsam Lake Mountain Wild Forest. This moderately rated 6-mile out-and-back hike features a steady climb with 1,197 feet of elevation gain, winding through hardwood forests before reaching the summit. At the top, hikers are rewarded with sweeping mountain views and a historic fire tower that offers a perfect spot to rest, refuel, and soak it all in. This trail is ideal for hikers looking for a solid challenge with a big payoff.
How do I sign up?

Register in the Student Recreation Center main office or over the phone (973-655-3340).
Cost for Students $10 / Faculty, Staff $20

Where and when?

Saturday, March 21, 2026
10:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m.
Begins at the Student Recreation Center

What to bring:Water, lunch/snacks, sunscreen, bug spray, layered clothing (depending on weather), sturdy close-toe hiking shoes or boots
Potential Hazards:Uneven and rocky terrain; steep sections; slippery surfaces (especially if wet); sudden weather changes; sun exposure; dehydration; insect bites; poisonous plants/allergies; wildlife encounters; and injuries including sprains/strains, abrasions, lacerations, and fractures.

Hosted by: Campus Recreation

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12265374

title: First Mountain Ramble w/ Sharon ~6 miles
description:
South Mountain Conservancy Activities Meetup Group
Saturday March 21st 8:00-11:00. First Mountain Ramble. 5-6 miles on trails depending on trail condition. Moderate pace and difficulty.

**Meet**: Locust Grove parking lot.

**Leader**: Sharon Salmon 973-715-5554.

\*\*\* In registering for this or any other South Mountain Conservancy (SMC) programs in 2026 online, I hereby release and agree to hold the SMC, its volunteers, staff and Board of Trustees, harmless from and waive on behalf of myself, my heirs, and any personal representatives any and all causes of action, claims, demands, damages, costs, expenses and compensation for damage or loss to myself and/or property that may be caused by any act, or failure to act, in this SMC activity, or that may otherwise arise in any way in connection with any other SMC activity. I understand that this release discharges the SMC from any liability or claim that I, my heirs, or any personal representatives, may have against the SMC with respect to any bodily injury, illness, death, medical treatment, or property damage that may arise from, or in connection to, any SMC program. This liability waiver and release extends to the SMC along with Essex County and its staff and any other affiliated organization or SMC partner. I also understand that by signing below I approve the possible use of photos of me at this SMC event on social media or in promotional materials.
**BY REGISTERING FOR THIS HIKE I attest I am in good health and attest that I: Am not experiencing any symptoms of illness such as cough, shortness of breath or difficulty breathing, fever, chills, repeated shaking with chills, muscle pain, headache, sore throat, or new loss of taste or smell.**
**Am following all CDC recommended guidelines, above, and limiting my exposure to the novel coronavirus/COVID-19.**
\# Arrival\, Weather\, & Cancellations
Please come to the starting point 10 minutes ahead of time so the hike can start promptly. Times are our best estimates; they can vary depending on conditions, and the size and pace of a group. If you have any questions, call the leader. **Temperatures above 90 degrees and steady rain cancels, but intermittent drizzle does not. You will *not* be notified.** **Bring water. Wear hiking boots, preferably waterproof with good soles and traction devices and bring poles (optional).** Dogs are not allowed on hikes unless specified.
These hikes are publicized first to SMC members and then advertised to the public one week before a hike. There is a limit of 25 participants, so pre-registration is required at least two days before a hike to sign the liability waiver online and allow lists to be sent to hike leaders. If you have registered and then find you cannot attend, please cancel your order online. **Repeated no shows without prior cancellation will result in removal from list.**
**To become a current member visit [[https://www.somocon.org/membership/](https://www.somocon.org/membership/)]([https://www.somocon.org/membership/](https://www.somocon.org/membership/)).**

title: Spring Birding Class - Migration
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
FREE Spring Birding Classes – Adults 
Explore the world of birds in the beautiful gardens of Van Vleck. Each class will focus on a specific topic to help us learn more about these amazing creatures. Weather permitting, a birding walk will be included so bring your binoculars (or borrow a pair from Van Vleck) and join us for some birding fun. Instructor: Naturalist Jenny Aley, Van Vleck Education Volunteer. 
March 21st – 9:00 AM -10:00 AM – Migration – Learn about migration and look for birds who are just passing through. 
Open to adults. Space is limited – register early.   
Free – Registration required – no drop-ins for this class. 
 
May 12th  – 9:00-10:00 AM – Birds and their Nests – Examine various bird nests and look at how birds prepare for their new families. 
June 27th – 9:00-10:00 AM – Young Birds – Sharpen your identification skills by focusing on young birds who don’t look exactly like adults yet.

title: Outdoor Adventures: Spring Has Sprung
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
March 21st – Spring has Sprung 
Saturday, 10:00 AM – 12:00 PM 
Join us as we celebrate the spring equinox at our March Outdoor Adventures program. Together we will explore the changes this season brings, what plants are starting to bloom in the gardens, and what wildlife we find in the springtime.   
On our Outdoor Adventures, you and your family explore and learn about the outdoor world through crafts, scavenger hunts, activity sheets and educational displays. Each program will focus on a specific theme, and our Van Vleck Education team will guide your family through this interactive learning experience. This program is entirely outdoors, weather dependent. Registration is required and includes everything you need to participate in the program. Designed for children ages 4 -11 but all are welcome. 
Fee: FREE for Members of Van Vleck; $15 per family  for  General Public 
Registration required by 1 day prior to each class 
 
Sponsored by  
  
Upcoming Outdoor Adventures 
April 11th – Arbor Day Everyday 
Arbor Day is a day to reflect on the importance of trees in our environment. We have an abundance of beautiful trees throughout the gardens. At April’s Outdoor Adventures, learn more about our trees as we explore their role in our environment, their unique leaf shapes, and what animals use trees as their homes.  
  
May 16th – Honeybees & Hummingbirds 
What do bees and hummingbirds have in common? They fly and they are pollinators! Join us to learn about your local pollinators, how they make a positive impact on our environment, and what we can do to help them on their mission.  
  
June 6th – Insect Safari  
You can find many different insects buzzing, wiggling, and crawling around the gardens. Join us for an Insect Safari at this month’s Outdoor Adventures. Create a bug inspired craft, take a hike looking for insects, and learn about the important roles insects play in the garden.

title: Inner Body Meditation
description:
The Mindful Stream
"Awareness of the inner body takes you beyond the mind." - Eckhart Tolle

In this mediation session we will bring our attention here: to the inner body. Sessions are suitable for newcomers, those experienced in Tolle's teachings and anyone wishing to remain completely anonymous. They will last around 20 minutes and cameras and microphones will be disabled.

(Depending on the situation, a free discussion may be held at the end with mics and cameras allowed.)

See you there!

Please be aware that sessions will close if no members have joined after 10 minutes.

Emrys also organises a free Work of Byron Katie online mediation group. Visit [this Meetup Group link](https://www.meetup.com/thework-runsfree/) for more information.

title: Rinc go deo! The DeNogla Academy of Irish Dance
description: Rinc go deo (dance forever)! Students from the DeNogla Academy of Irish Dance will keep the St. Patrick’s Day celebrations going with a performance of riveting Irish Dance! The DeNogla Academy of Irish Dance has been home to regional, national, and world champion Irish dancers since 1993. No registration required.

title: Black Women in Biopic: Queen of Katwe (2016)
description:
Queen of Katwe (2016) | PG, 124mins

This biopic of Ugandan chess prodigy Phiona Mutesi traces her journey from the slums of Katwe, where she is forced to abandon her schooling at the age of nine, to the upper echelons of the chess world after she develops an interest in the game at a youth-outreach program. Watch the trailer here.


Seating for the film is first come, first served. No registration is required.

title: Tadhana: MUFASA’s 2026 Semiformal
description:
Join us for a night written in the stars!
Tadhana, the Filipino word for destiny and fate, is the unseen force that guides our stories, even when we don’t realize it. MUFASA invites you to step into that magic at our annual cultural ball inspired by precolonial Filipino mythology.
Come dressed as a deity, monster, or myth that speaks to you. This is a night for self-expression, creativity, and honoring our heritage while choosing how we show up in the present.
While we celebrate Filipino culture at the heart of Tadhana, all cultures, identities, and backgrounds are wholeheartedly welcome. Whether you’re channeling an ancestor, a legend, or your own destiny, there is space for you at Tadhana.

Hosted by: Montclair Unified Filipino American Student Association

title: Cultural ARTS Series: New Jersey Symphony Chamber Trio
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Tonight’s featured performers hail from the distinguished New Jersey Symphony (NJS), which has been enriching communities through music since 1922. The NJS gave its very first concert right here in Montclair and has since evolved into a nationally recognized orchestra known for its artistic excellence and deep community engagement. 
This concert will feature a woodwind trio: 
   
Bart Feller, flute                   Bob Ingliss, oboe           Juan Esteban Martinez, clarinet 
 
Program Details 
Claude Debussy – Syrinx, for solo flute 
Antonio Vivaldi – Concerto in G Minor 
Malcolm Arnold – Divertimento for flute, oboe, clarinet 
Alberto Ginastera – Duo for flute and oboe 
Robert Muczyinski – Duo for flute and clarinet 
Johann Joachim Quantz – Trio Sonata in C Minor 
Tickets are required. There will be wine and cheese and a chance to ask the musicians questions.

title: Interfaith Community Conversation
description:
Join Rabbi Julie and President Aaron Delaney in a series of discussions around the Interfaith Community.

Rabbi Julie will be hosting a series of three community meetings in February and March, where she will present background information about interfaith families, including about the demographics of our communities and the policies of the Conservative Movement on this issue. Participants in these meetings will be invited to give input and engage in dialogue with the Rabbi, Aaron Delaney, and with other members of the community as we share perspectives around this topic.

Babysitting will be available at the Sunday sessions upon request (email Aviva Arad aarad@shomrei.org).

The dates of the Community Meetings are as follows:

Sunday, February 22, 10:00 – 11:30am

Tuesday, March 3, 8:00 – 9:30pm, Zoom Only (https://shomrei.org/z\_rabbijulie: https://shomrei.org/z_rabbijulie)

Sunday, March 22, 1:00 – 2:30pm at the social hall

description:

Mondays, 9:30 AM – 10:45 AM 
Instructor: Jennifer Hondru-Celestin 
Join our welcoming community for a meditative yoga flow practice in the Van Vleck living room overlooking the charming courtyard gardens. All levels of yoga experience are welcome in this dynamic, inclusive class. title: Free Swim Lessons *Beginner*
description:
Brief Description: 
Never swam before? No problem! These lessons will give you the important foundational skills in swimming and help you be more comfortable in the pool. Learn how to use proper breath control, float, and swim with a kickboard. 
Skills Focus:

Being comfortable in the water
Learning how to float on your back and front
Learning how to kick off the wall with a kickboard
Introducing proper breath control in the water
Learning how to tread water
Learn alternate kicks (Froggy Kick)
Being comfortable in the deep end 
Introducing arms to your strokes

How do I Sign-up?

FREE with a valid Montclair State ID

description:
Journaling in the Garden 
Monthly on Mondays, 12:00 PM – 1:00 PM. (Series or individual Classes) 
March 23rd 
April 13th 
May 11th 
June 15th 
Explore the beauty of the Van Vleck Gardens this spring through reflective writing and creative mark-making.  This mindfulness-based class/series invites you to slow down, observe the natural world and turn your blank pages into a wonderland of personal observations.  Each class takes place in a different area of the Gardens (weather permitting), offering new inspiration for discovery.  Deepen your connection with nature and yourself in a no-pressure environment.  No art or writing experience necessary – just an openness to explore, reflect and create.   
Fee: Series Rate (4 classes): $50 Members / $70 General Public 
Individual Rate: $15 Members / $20 General Public 
Registration required by 1 day prior to class.

title: GOVERNING BEYOND ELECTIONS: ACCOUNTABILITY, PROSPERITY AND THE AMERICAN ADMINISTRATIVE STATE
location: Presentation Hall (College of Communication and Media)
description:
The emergence of a highly competent bureaucracy is typically under appreciated, if not ignored, as an ingredient in the emergence of the American experiment. While government administrators are often derided as inefficient or even obstructionist, technocrats are responsible for making government work and offer policy continuity and expertise across administrations. Still, the growth of a large permanent bureaucracy challenges the notion of accountability grounded in democratic elections. By tracing historical developments and contemporary debates, this lecture focuses on the centrality of government administrative capacity in the American experiment, focusing on the tensions and collaboration between elected officials and professional bureaucrats. We will consider the role of independent government agencies that were established with the idea of partitioning regulatory functions from partisan pressure.

Hosted by: Campus Activities, Civic & Voter Engagement

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12050870

title: 2026 Class Gift Tabling - Information and Philanthropy Cord Pick-Up
description:
Each year, our graduating class participates in a proud Class Gift tradition where members of the class invest in the University by supporting areas of Montclair State University that are most important to them. We invite the Class of 2026 to come together to show your support!
All gifts to Montclair count! Make your gift to any area of campus you feel most passionate about – such as the Emergency Book Fund, The Red Hawk Pantry, your school or college, The Fund for Montclair State or athletic program.
Your gift will pay it forward for future Red Hawks and kick-start your philanthropic connection to Montclair.

Commemorate your class year! Make a gift of at least $20.26 and you’ll receive your customized philanthropy cord to wear over your commencement robe. 
For a gift of $40, celebrate this milestone with your family, friends and colleagues by having your message displayed on the screen at the Commencement you will be participating in, and receive a customized philanthropy cord.

How do I get my customized philanthropy cord?
Customized philanthropy cords will be available for pick-up:

Tuesday, October 28 from 1:00 – 3:00 p.m. in the Student Center Lobby
Thursday, November 20  from 1:00 – 3:00 p.m. in the Student Center Lobby
Tuesday, December 2 from 12:00 – 3:00 p.m. in the Student Center Lobby
Bloomfield Campus Graduation Fair: Wednesday, December 10th from 12:00 – 6:00 p.m. in Deacon’s Den, Talbott Hall (for Bloomfield graduates only)
Montclair Campus Graduation Fairs: Thursday, December 11th from 10:00 a.m. – 7:00 p.m. in the Student Center Ballrooms (for Montclair or Bloomfield graduates)

Graduates can also arrange to pick up their philanthropy cords in the Overlook Corporate Center, 150 Clove Road, Little Falls, NJ during business hours, Monday through Friday, 8:30 a.m. – 4:30 p.m. Philanthropy cords can also be mailed to those who cannot pick it up. The last day we will mail the cords is Monday, December 22, 2025 at 2:30 p.m. Please contact James Stewart, Associate Director of Annual Giving at stewartj@montclair.edu or 973-655-5328 to finalize options to get your cords for the Winter Commencement.
Every student has benefitted from donor generosity to areas of academic support, emergency funding for students and overall priorities to boost students’ success every day. Thank you for paying it forward!For more information, please contact James Stewart, Associate Director of Annual Giving, at 973-655-5328 or stewartj@montclair.edu.

title: Understanding Rights and Responsibilities— Immigration Policy
description:
NEW DATE FOR SESSIONS! 
As immigration policies develop, it is best to stay educated. Know your rights and responsibilities!
Join the New Jersey Consortium for Immigrant Children for a webinar on key legal and policy issues impacting the Montclair community. Information will be provided on immigration statuses, policy updates, legal protections, judicial vs. administrative orders, and understanding your rights if encountering law enforcement. Sessions are open for students, family, faculty, and staff in both English and Spanish. 
The New Jersey Consortium for Immigrant Children (NJCIC) is a state-wide legal and policy advocacy and education organization specializing in youth immigration. They provide objective legal resources, aid, and advice on immigration rights.

title: The DJ Academy: Music Matters 101
description:
DJing is one of the original elements of Hip Hop and is often considered the first. Hip Hop began in the Bronx during the 1970s at community block parties, where DJs used turntables to bring people together through music. Early pioneers like DJ Kool Herc introduced breakbeats by looping the most rhythmic parts of records, creating the foundation for breaking and shaping how dancers and MCs interacted with the music.
As Hip Hop evolved, DJing became the musical backbone of the culture. DJs provide the beats for MCing, set the tempo for dancers, and control the energy of Hip Hop spaces by reading the crowd and responding in real time. Through techniques like scratching, cutting, and beat juggling, DJs helped define Hip Hop’s sound while preserving its history and passing down cultural knowledge.
This class introduces the basics of DJing and how music shapes the dance experience. DJ Bascis will explain core concepts such as tempo, rhythm, and song structure, helping students understand how music guides movement and energy on the dance floor.
Participants will learn how DJs choose music, create smooth transitions, and adjust the vibe based on the room. 
Through simple examples and live demonstrations, students will build confidence in listening to music with intention. This can bewhether they are dancing, freestyling, or interested in DJing themselves.
This class is designed for beginners and college students, with no prior DJ or music experience required.

Hosted by: Campus Activities at Bloomfield

title: Virtual Meditation
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Virtual Meditation 
Instructor: John Welshons 
Wednesdays, 7:00 PM – 9:00 PM 
January 7, 14, 21, 28 
February 4, 11, 25 
March 4, 18, 25 
April 1, 8, 15, 22, 29 
Join contemporary spiritual teacher and author Ramananda John E. Welshons (One Soul, One Love, One Heart, When Prayers Aren’t Answered and Awakening From Grief) for a series of evening meditation classes in the comfort of your own home via Zoom. These classes will focus on specific practices for working with fear and anxiety.  Participants will learn and practice different forms of mindfulness meditation in each session and will have the opportunity to participate in discussions of Ramananda’s three critically acclaimed books. Open to beginners and experienced practitioners, these gatherings offer an opportunity to experience deep inner peace while practicing with a highly experienced meditation teacher.  
Important Updated Registration Information: 
A Zoom Link will be sent the day of the class. If you are registering after 3 PM on the day of the class, the Zoom link will be included in the automated payment confirmation email sent when you register. There is no registration deadline for this class. 
Fee: Single class: $10 Members / $15 General Public

title: 2026 Employment Law Update & To-Do Checklist

title: Champions for the Arts Donna Walker-Kuhne
description:
With the loss of “third spaces,” an increase in online socialization, and the dwindling of social clubs and groups, we are increasingly seeking a way to combat loneliness and isolation. Through their ability to bring people together in person and to connect our hearts to beat as one, the arts remain a powerful way to bring us closer to those around us. Donna Walker-Kuhne, the nation’s foremost expert in audience development, leads an inspiring discussion on how artistic communities can meaningfully connect, grow inclusive audiences, and create a more vibrant artistic world. In this open and inviting conversation, she shares concrete examples of successful audience outreach from her extensive field experience, as well as spotlighting young Champions for the Arts in the Montclair State University community. They’re ready to pledge their commitment to forging dynamic strategies to strengthen the arts. Are you ready to join them? 
 
Donna Walker-Kuhne is an award-winning thought leader, lawyer, scholar, educator, writer, and strategist for community engagement, audience development, and social justice. Acknowledged as the nation‘s foremost expert on audience development by the Arts & Business Council, she has worked with NJPAC, Dance Theatre of Harlem, Alvin Ailey American Dance Theater, The Public Theater, WNYC Radio, The Apollo Theater, and more, and consulted on over 22 Broadway productions. As founder and president of Walker International Communications Group, Inc., she has raised over $23 million in earned income by promoting the arts to diverse communities. Walker is the author of Invitation to the Party, Building Bridges to Arts, Culture, and Community, and Champions for the Arts, Successful Strategies for Engaging Dynamic Communities.

title: CALI SCHOOL OF MUSIC | Colin Carr Cello Masterclass
description:
Colin Carr Cello Masterclass
Internationally acclaimed cellist Colin Carr, renowned as a soloist, chamber musician, recording artist, and dedicated teacher, will visit the Cali School for a masterclass and share his insights into cello performance and musical artistry.
Bio
Colin Carr appears throughout the world as a soloist, chamber musician, recording artist, and teacher. He has played with major orchestras worldwide, including the Royal Concertgebouw Orchestra, The Philharmonia, Royal Philharmonic, BBC Symphony, the orchestras of Chicago, Los Angeles, Washington, Philadelphia, Montréal and all the major orchestras of Australia and New Zealand. Conductors with whom he has worked include Rattle, Gergiev, Dutoit, Elder, Skrowasczewski and Marriner. Chamber music plays an important role in his musical life. He is a frequent visitor to international festivals and has appeared often as a guest with the Guarneri and Emerson string quartets and with New York's Chamber Music Society of Lincoln Center. He has held teaching positions at the New England Conservatory and the Royal Academy of Music. St John’s College, Oxford created the post of “Musician in Residence” for him. Since 2002 he been a professor at Stony Brook University in New York.

Hosted by: The College of the Arts

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12290610

title: Let's Walk!! in Belleville Park
description:
Let's Walk!!
Let’s get out and enjoy nature by walking about 3 miles in Belleville Park.

We will also check out the nearby Essex Hudson Greenway and Branch Brook Park. Wear comfortable shoes and bring some water!

Belleville Park is a mid-sized, 32.70-acre park designed by the Olmsteds with a playground, bocce courts, ball fields & a jogging path. It is bordered by Belleville and Parkside Avenues and Mill Street along the Second River, which was once a part of the Morris Canal.

**We start promptly, so please arrive at least 5 minutes before the start.**
Rain or extreme weather will cancel the event.

Disclaimer: If you join us for a hike/walk, please understand that you are responsible for your own preparedness and well-being and will hold no one else liable in case of injury or mishap.

title: Orange & Tulip Springs w/ Donna ~5 miles
description:
South Mountain Conservancy Activities Meetup Group
Meet 9:30 at Tulip Springs. 6 Mile hike lower Orange to upper Orange and then some.

Bring snacks, water, poles, spikes if needed. Moderate pace.

**Leader**: Donna Burkat- 973-454-5635 call or text if you need to cancel.

\*\*\* In registering for this or any other South Mountain Conservancy (SMC) programs in 2026 online, I hereby release and agree to hold the SMC, its volunteers, staff and Board of Trustees, harmless from and waive on behalf of myself, my heirs, and any personal representatives any and all causes of action, claims, demands, damages, costs, expenses and compensation for damage or loss to myself and/or property that may be caused by any act, or failure to act, in this SMC activity, or that may otherwise arise in any way in connection with any other SMC activity. I understand that this release discharges the SMC from any liability or claim that I, my heirs, or any personal representatives, may have against the SMC with respect to any bodily injury, illness, death, medical treatment, or property damage that may arise from, or in connection to, any SMC program. This liability waiver and release extends to the SMC along with Essex County and its staff and any other affiliated organization or SMC partner. I also understand that by signing below I approve the possible use of photos of me at this SMC event on social media or in promotional materials.
**BY REGISTERING FOR THIS HIKE I attest I am in good health and attest that I: Am not experiencing any symptoms of illness such as cough, shortness of breath or difficulty breathing, fever, chills, repeated shaking with chills, muscle pain, headache, sore throat, or new loss of taste or smell.**
**Am following all CDC recommended guidelines, above, and limiting my exposure to the novel coronavirus/COVID-19.**
\# Arrival\, Weather\, & Cancellations
Please come to the starting point 10 minutes ahead of time so the hike can start promptly. Times are our best estimates; they can vary depending on conditions, and the size and pace of a group. If you have any questions, call the leader. **Temperatures above 90 degrees and steady rain cancels, but intermittent drizzle does not. You will *not* be notified.** **Bring water. Wear hiking boots, preferably waterproof with good soles and traction devices and bring poles (optional).** Dogs are not allowed on hikes unless specified.
These hikes are publicized first to SMC members and then advertised to the public one week before a hike. There is a limit of 25 participants, so pre-registration is required at least two days before a hike to sign the liability waiver online and allow lists to be sent to hike leaders. If you have registered and then find you cannot attend, please cancel your order online. **Repeated no shows without prior cancellation will result in removal from list.**
**To become a current member visit [[https://www.somocon.org/membership/](https://www.somocon.org/membership/)]([https://www.somocon.org/membership/](https://www.somocon.org/membership/)).**

title: Ping Pong Party - North Jersey
description:
North Jersey Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis And More (BVT)
We're a group of people who will casually hit the ball around and rotate playing to 10 or 15 points for two to three hours. If there's an interest, we may do king of the hill, round robin or set up a small tournament.

All are welcome!! It's just casual fun! Come anytime! We're not in a league and are not here to analyze your form.

We'll be heading indoors to the table tennis tables at Ready to Golf, 3 New Bridge Road in River Edge. We'll likely have pizza delivered or hang out some more at Coach House Diner, 55 Rte 4 in Hackensack.

Ready to Golf has a lot to offer, including rentable table tennis tables, and comes as a recommendation of Joe, one of our many regulars.

We'll be reserving four or five tables for singles and doubles play but will get more if they're available and more than 20 people show up.

The rental price covers paddles and balls. Final pricing will be somewhere in the neighborhood of $10-15 per person. Please bring cash to pay for what you owe! We'll settle the bill before play.

For planning purposes, we also ask that if you're stopping by later than 8 p.m., that you please let me know by commenting below, or calling or texting me.

There is a snack bar on site, but it's only open until 7:30 p.m. There are some bottled drinks available and a water fountain, but you're welcome to bring food and beverages from the outside. It's BYOB friendly. There's also plenty of seating and space for hanging out on the side when not playing and some televisions with sports programming.

If interested in learning more about the place, here's the website: www.readytogolf.com

Hope you'll join us for our fifth ping pong event. Questions, comments, concerns or having trouble finding us? You can message Andy Milone on Meetup, or call/text him at 201-234-9609.

If interested, check out one of our many other events. We do bowling, volleyball, tennis, soccer, hiking, martial arts, billiards, dancing and bar crawls: [www.meetup.com/north-jersey-bowling-soccer-volleyball](https://www.meetup.com/north-jersey-bowling-soccer-volleyball)
source: Meetup: NJ Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis & More

title: Writing as Spiritual Practice Covenant Group
description:
Are you interested in exploring the monthly themes through writing and sharing with others? The purpose of this group is to write about the monthly theme before the meeting and share your writing with the group. Facilitated by Dennis Kurtti, it will be held each month on the fourth Saturday. This is not a writing critique group, and all levels of writing experience are welcome. If you would like to understand more about this kind of covenant group, you can watch the worship service held on 8/21/22, which featured speakers from an already existing group. To express your interest in this group, contact Laura Wilson at CovenantGroups@uumontclair.org

title: Mindfulness in the Garden (Weekend Classes)
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Mindfulness in the Garden (Weekend Classes) 
Saturdays, 9:30 AM – 10:30 AM 
March 28th 
April 18th 
May 9th 
June 13th 
Instructor: Jodi Brooks 
Discover how a mindfulness practice can help us awaken and  live in harmony with oneself and with the natural world. Each class will include: simple instructions for getting started with a formal mindfulness practice, very gentle stretching & breathwork, and an outdoor contemplative garden walk. This is a practice of slowing down, cultivating quiet and paying attention to our senses. All levels are welcome. No experience needed. Cold or rainy weather – class will be held indoors, with a short walk outdoors (weather permitting). 
Fee per class:  $20 Members / $25 General Public 
Pre Registration is required.  There are no drop-ins for this class.

title: Casual Volleyball - North Jersey
description:
North Jersey Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis And More (BVT)
We're a group of people who will start by hitting the ball around for the first 20-30 minutes and then will break up into 2-4 teams for two or three hours of play.

If we have more than 20 people show up, we'll break up into three or four teams of 6 (Some may have 7, 8 or 9. Additional players just rotate into play). Games will be to 10 points. Rally scoring. Winner stays on (unless a team has won four straight).

We'll be heading outdoors to the hard court in Leonia off Overpeck County Pk Driveway in the Overpeck County Park.

\*\*\* After playing, we'll be likely heading to Tropical Juice Bar, an idea of regular attendee Kate, for a drink and snack. It's located at 60 River St. in Hackensack. Feel free to join us or suggest another spot for a post-volleyball hangout. \*\*\*

There's no fee to play volleyball and nothing else you need to do besides show up. Come anytime!! All are welcome!!! We're not in a league and are not here to analyze your form. We're more for people who haven't played in months, maybe years, and just want to occasionally play again and hang out.

Directions: You can Google "Volleyball Court in Leonia" and it should come up. Or type 41 Willow Tree Road, Leonia, NJ 07605 into your GPS, but note that it's not on Willow Tree Road, just near it. Instead, turn onto Overpeck County Pk Driveway off Fort Lee Road (Degraw Avenue). It is past the tennis courts and near a basketball court and track. You can try Googling Leonia High School as well. That should help you find it.

We don't have the court reserved and if it happens to be taken, we may play with those already occupying it or we will try to relocate on the fly. It's certainly possible that we may have to cancel that day if we can't find a suitable spot. Please keep that in mind. And if it's looking like it'll rain or snow, I'll make a call on whether to cancel around an hour ahead of time.

Please bring a ball and net if you have them for possible use during the game. Feel free to bring a speaker for music, or snacks and drinks for consumption during play.

If you have trouble finding the court or group, you can call or text Andy Milone at 201-234-9609. There is plenty of parking and an open bathroom at the park.

Hope you'll join us for our 17th volleyball event. Questions or comments? You can message Andy on Meetup, or call/text him at 201-234-9609.

Check out bowling, soccer, volleyball, tennis or one of our other activities at: www.meetup.com/north-jersey-bowling-soccer-volleyball
source: Meetup: NJ Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis & More

title: Patriotic Trivia (Bloomfield Campus)
description:
Stars, stripes, and some serious smarts! ????? Join us for Patriotic Trivia, a fast-paced, fun-filled event where you can test your knowledge of U.S. history, government, iconic moments, and all things red, white, and blue!
Whether you’re a history buff or just in it for the fun, this is your chance to show off what you know, learn something new, and enjoy some friendly competition with fellow students, faculty, and staff.
From the Constitution to pop culture, the questions will keep you guessing—and prizes are up for grabs! Come solo or bring a team, and get ready for an exciting celebration of civic knowledge and national pride.
Think you’ve got what it takes? Let’s find out—one trivia question at a time! Stop by, enjoy some pizza and you might even win some really cool prizes

Hosted by: Civic & Voter Engagement, Campus Activities at Bloomfield

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12266498

location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Early Spring Session: March 2, 9, 16, 23, 30, April 6, 13, 20, 27 
Mondays, 9:30 AM – 10:45 AM 
Instructor: Jennifer Hondru-Celestin 
Join our welcoming community for a meditative yoga flow practice in the Van Vleck living room overlooking the charming courtyard gardens. All levels of yoga experience are welcome in this dynamic, inclusive class. Our teacher Jennifer Hondru-Celestin takes inspiration from the natural beauty around us to flow with the seasons, blending mindful movement, breath, meditation, and a good dose of humor and fun.  This class welcomes all levels from beginner to experienced yogis. Bring your own yoga mat and props. 
Fee:  Single class: $23 Members / $28 Non-Members 
Late Winter Series (6 classes): $120 Members/ $150 General Public

title: Free Swim Lessons *Beginner*
description:
Brief Description: 
Never swam before? No problem! These lessons will give you the important foundational skills in swimming and help you be more comfortable in the pool. Learn how to use proper breath control, float, and swim with a kickboard. 
Skills Focus:

Being comfortable in the water
Learning how to float on your back and front
Learning how to kick off the wall with a kickboard
Introducing proper breath control in the water
Learning how to tread water
Learn alternate kicks (Froggy Kick)
Being comfortable in the deep end 
Introducing arms to your strokes

How do I Sign-up?

Registration is not required
FREE with a valid Montclair State ID

When and Where?

Monday: 1-2PM & 5-6PM
Student Recreation Center - Pool 


Hosted by: Campus Recreation

Swimming unassisted on your front and back 
Improve treading (in the deep end)

title: Bench Press and Squat Competition
description:
Spring Strength Shadow! Welcome back to our annual Bench Press & Squat Competition. Come and test power and show your gains. Each event will feature 5 reps max. Whether you're chasing PRs or just want to flex for fun, this event is for you! Bring your friends and your lifting straps, and see who can lift their way to the top prize!
How do I Sign-up?

Registration is not required
FREE with a valid Montclair State ID

When and Where?

Data: March 30th
Time: 3pm - 6pm
Student Recreation Center, Plae Room

How are results scored

Maximum 5 repetitions 
1 attempt per lift after a warm up set
Scoring calculation: Weight lifted x repetitions divided by body weight 
Final score will be a total of both lifts combined 

Prizes: 

Will be given to the top 3 winners 
Tier 1:Beat Headphone, Fitness Watch, Air Fryer $100-$200
Tier 2: Massage Gun, Meal Prep Kit, Pilates Kit $50-$100
Tier 3: Foam Roller Set, Liquid IV, Owala Water Bottle $20-$50

Free T-shirts - limited supply so show up early to claim one!

Hosted by: Campus Recreation

title: Mindfulness in the Garden - Early Spring Series
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Mindfulness in the Garden (Weekday Series) 
Instructor: Jodi Brooks 
Tuesdays, 11:00 AM – 12:00 PM 
Early Spring Session: Mar 24, Apr 7, 14, & 28 (No class 4/21) 
Discover how a mindfulness practice can help us awaken and  live in harmony with oneself and with the natural world. This class will include: simple instructions for getting started with a formal mindfulness practice, very gentle stretching & breathwork, and an outdoor contemplative garden walk. This is a practice of slowing down, cultivating quiet and paying attention to our senses. All levels are welcome. No experience needed. Winter session will be held indoors, with a short walk outdoors (weather permitting). 
Fee:  Each 4-class Series: $80 Members / $100 General Public 
Series registration only. Pre Registration is required – there are no drop-ins for this class. 
 
Late Spring Session: May 5, 12 19 & 26 
Early Summer Session: June 2, 9, 16 & 23

title: The DJ Academy: Music Matters 101
description:
DJing is one of the original elements of Hip Hop and is often considered the first. Hip Hop began in the Bronx during the 1970s at community block parties, where DJs used turntables to bring people together through music. Early pioneers like DJ Kool Herc introduced breakbeats by looping the most rhythmic parts of records, creating the foundation for breaking and shaping how dancers and MCs interacted with the music.
As Hip Hop evolved, DJing became the musical backbone of the culture. DJs provide the beats for MCing, set the tempo for dancers, and control the energy of Hip Hop spaces by reading the crowd and responding in real time. Through techniques like scratching, cutting, and beat juggling, DJs helped define Hip Hop’s sound while preserving its history and passing down cultural knowledge.
This class introduces the basics of DJing and how music shapes the dance experience. DJ Bascis will explain core concepts such as tempo, rhythm, and song structure, helping students understand how music guides movement and energy on the dance floor.
Participants will learn how DJs choose music, create smooth transitions, and adjust the vibe based on the room. 
Through simple examples and live demonstrations, students will build confidence in listening to music with intention. This can bewhether they are dancing, freestyling, or interested in DJing themselves.
This class is designed for beginners and college students, with no prior DJ or music experience required.

Hosted by: Campus Activities at Bloomfield

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12095721

title: Virtual Meditation
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Virtual Meditation 
Instructor: John Welshons 
Wednesdays, 7:00 PM – 9:00 PM 
January 7, 14, 21, 28 
February 4, 11, 25 
March 4, 18, 25 
April 1, 8, 15, 22, 29 
Join contemporary spiritual teacher and author Ramananda John E. Welshons (One Soul, One Love, One Heart, When Prayers Aren’t Answered and Awakening From Grief) for a series of evening meditation classes in the comfort of your own home via Zoom. These classes will focus on specific practices for working with fear and anxiety.  Participants will learn and practice different forms of mindfulness meditation in each session and will have the opportunity to participate in discussions of Ramananda’s three critically acclaimed books. Open to beginners and experienced practitioners, these gatherings offer an opportunity to experience deep inner peace while practicing with a highly experienced meditation teacher.  
Important Updated Registration Information: 
A Zoom Link will be sent the day of the class. If you are registering after 3 PM on the day of the class, the Zoom link will be included in the automated payment confirmation email sent when you register. There is no registration deadline for this class. 
Fee: Single class: $10 Members / $15 General Public

title: Eagle Rock Reservation
description:
Let's Walk!!
JOIN US FOR A WALK. WE WALK AT A MODERATE PACE ON UNPAVED PATHS.

We will hike for about an hour - 3.5 miles. We will be walking on trails in the woods, so appropriate footwear, long pants & socks, and bug spray.

Will will meet at:
[https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7](https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7) Eagle Rock Trail Parking Lot by Kiosk#1

If you enter from Eagle Rock Way, go pass the Highlawn and stay left. There will be a parking lot at the end of the curve to your right.

Eagle Rock Reservation is 408 acres of hills, streams, and valleys located on the borders of West Orange, Verona, and Montclair, New Jersey. Though located in the middle of the most densely populated county in the most densely populated state in the country, the Reservation is mostly undeveloped and boasts an extensive trail system within its borders.

**Please make sure to RSVP and arrive 5 minutes prior to start time.**

FOLLOW US ON INSTAGRAM - letswalkmeetup

Disclaimer: If you join us for a hike, please understand that you are responsible for your own preparedness and well-being and will hold no one else liable in case of injury or mishap. Event will be canceled in the event of extreme weather.

title: Bowling Night - North Jersey
description:
North Jersey Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis And More (BVT)
This is an event for casual bowling at Montvale Lanes, 14 W. Grand Ave. All are welcome for **two games, food and drinks**, and any post- or pre-game hang out. Show up anytime! Questions or comments? You can message Andy Milone on Meetup, or call/text him at **201-234-9609**.

\*\*\* Before bowling, we might stop by the Pizza Parlour, 6 S. Kinderkamack Road in Montvale, for a few slices at 6:15 p.m. First, post a comment on the event page to gauge interest. **After bowling, we'll definitely be heading to Davey's Irish Pub & Restaurant, 5 Park St. in Montvale, or The Ridge Diner, 125 Kinderkamack Road in Park Ridge, at 10 p.m. for some food and/or drinks up until midnight.**\*\*\*

About the group: We're not professionals and we're not in a league. In fact, we're a group of people who probably each won't score more than 100 points and will land more than our fair share of gutter balls. There's no pressure at all!!!! We're not here to analyze your form or technique.

More event details: You pay for your own games, food and drinks. Please bring cash. The alley does not accept card. But there is an ATM on site. The **cost per player is $18 for two games** and bowling shoes. If you have your own shoes, the cost is $12. Please try to bring exact change since Andy will have a limited amount of cash. If you bring a $20 bill and Andy doesn't have change, any remainder would just go toward the bowling alley tip.

Food and drinks (alcoholic and non-alcoholic) are available on site, plus tables and an alley bar to hang at during the event. There is a dart board and a few arcade games, as well.

**What do you do when you get there?** First, find the group inside around the front desk. Look for a growing group of people! Call or text Andy Milone at 201-234-9609 if you can't find us. Pay Andy before getting bowling shoes. **\*NEW\*** He'll give you a name tag or wrist band. **\*NEW\***

**Please know that it is possible we have to wait 20-40 minutes until lane(s) free up. (He is thinking about changes to possibly address this too).** It's certainly possible that we may have to relocate or cancel that night if there's a long wait list upon arrival. Please keep that in mind if traveling from far away!

For those who don't have cars or are looking to avoid driving, the Montvale Train Station is only like a five-minute walk from the bowling alley and can be reached via the Pascack Valley Line. Last train leaves at 12:20 a.m. and heads toward Hoboken. (https://gmtma.org/wp-content/uploads/2011/11/rail_system_map.jpg)

Many thanks to Taylor, one of our group participants, for recommending Montvale Lanes! You can learn more at: www.montvalelanes.com.

Hope you'll join us for our 22nd bowling event, or consider attending one of our volleyball, ping pong, skating or other activities -> www.meetup.com/north-jersey-bowling-soccer-volleyball
source: Meetup: NJ Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis & More

South Mountain Conservancy Activities Meetup Group
**TRAIL WORK** will begin this year with finishing work on the Lenape Trail reroute below the Beech Brook Cascades. The steps are in (see photo), but final tread improvements on switchbacks and throughout are needed. We will meet at the Dog Park (11 Bear Lane) and head down to the Lenape trail from there.

Trail work is physically challenging but very satisfying as we use hand tools to build and repair trails. Participants must be 15+ years. Those younger than 18 must be accompanied by an adult. Participants should wear long pants due to [ticks](https://www.somocon.org/?page_id=193928&fb) and sturdy shoes, and bring gloves (though backup gloves will be available). Tools, water, and a snack will be provided. (Registration is not required though if you sign up on Meetup, it helps us know how many tools to bring.)

title: Awakening from Grief
description:
Awakening from Grief: Finding the Way back to Joy 
April 7th & 14th, 7:00 PM – 9:30 PM 
Instructor: John Welshons 
Van Vleck House is pleased to present AWAKENING FROM GRIEF: Finding the Way Back to Joy – one of our most popular workshops. Led by John E. Welshons, and based on his critically acclaimed book of the same title, AWAKENING FROM GRIEF offers the opportunity to explore – and heal – the psychological, emotional and spiritual challenges that follow in the wake of a loss or unexpected change. This workshop helps participants to discover the inner resources that will aid them in working through life’s inevitable challenges. We will gather in the living room of the Van Vleck Main House. Our time together will combine lecture, discussion, and a variety of meditative exercises. Participants complete the workshop feeling a profound sense of relief . . . and hope. Reading Awakening From Grief prior to the class is recommended, though not a requirement. 
Fee: Series (includes 2 classes) $65 for Members $80 for General Public 
Pre Registration is required by Monday, April 6th.  There are no drop-ins for this class.

title: Midday MeetUp - Jews and Baseball, A Love Affair
description:
**Midday MeetUp - Jews and Baseball, A Love Affair**  
  
A monthly gathering to make connections and learn together.  
11:45am Catered Lunch (free)  
12:30pm Featured Program   
  
Join baseball historian Sam Bernstein for a lively survey of Jewish involvement in America’s National Pastime.  
  
Cost:** FREE or Suggested Donation of $10 per person to offset costs. (Bill My Account is only open to Shomrei Members)  
  
**RSVP:** Required so we know how much food to buy.  
https://shomrei.easyshul.com/events/6967e303368f110026c0c410  
  
**\***Members & PreSchool Families should use their fob to access the building.  
  
**About the Program**  
Using audio and video, *Jews and Baseball* explores the origins of the game and the impact Jewish players, owners, and innovators have had on its evolution. The program highlights figures such as Hank Greenberg, Sandy Koufax, Barney Dreyfuss, Bud Selig, Lou Boudreau, Marvin Miller, as well as the game's current Jewish stars. It also delves into topics including antisemitism in the sport, baseball’s growth in Israel, and the lore of the “Koufax Curse.”  
  
**About Sam Bernstein**  
Sam Bernstein is a retired Social Worker who has published many articles on baseball history. He received a BA in History from the American University and an MSW from Adelphi University. After a career serving the Jewish community at JCCs and Jewish Family Service, Sam worked for the Elizabeth Public Schools and as an Instructor in Social Work for Rutgers University. He was raised in Malverne, NY and moved to New Jersey with his wife Maureen in 1999.  
  
Full_Season_Schedule_for_Midday_MeetUps: https://shomrei.org/calendar/midday-meetup-calendar  
  
**Questions?** Contact June Zimmerman,   
VP for Programming  
jzimmerman@shomrei.org  
  
\#middaymeetup

title: Eagle Rock Reservation
description:
Let's Walk!!
JOIN US FOR A WALK. WE WALK AT A MODERATE PACE ON UNPAVED PATHS.

We will hike for about an hour - 3.5 miles. We will be walking on trails in the woods, so appropriate footwear, long pants & socks, and bug spray.

Will will meet at:
[https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7](https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7) Eagle Rock Trail Parking Lot by Kiosk#1

If you enter from Eagle Rock Way, go pass the Highlawn and stay left. There will be a parking lot at the end of the curve to your right.

Eagle Rock Reservation is 408 acres of hills, streams, and valleys located on the borders of West Orange, Verona, and Montclair, New Jersey. Though located in the middle of the most densely populated county in the most densely populated state in the country, the Reservation is mostly undeveloped and boasts an extensive trail system within its borders.

**Please make sure to RSVP and arrive 5 minutes prior to start time.**

FOLLOW US ON INSTAGRAM - letswalkmeetup

Disclaimer: If you join us for a hike, please understand that you are responsible for your own preparedness and well-being and will hold no one else liable in case of injury or mishap. Event will be canceled in the event of extreme weather.

title: EARTH WEEK CELEBRATION 2026
description:
The Northeast Earth Coalition (NEEC) invites you to join the 2026 Earth Week Celebration April 18-26, for a week of events on the theme of “OUR POWER OUR PLANET.” Between Saturday April 18 and Sunday April 26, we will have volunteer opportunities for cleanups in parks, public spaces, and community gardens. 
The Northeast Earth Coalition is partnering with the Township of Montclair, the Parks and Recreation Committee, Friends of Montclair Parks, and The Brookdale Park Conservancy. 
 
  
THE NEEC IS PART OF THE GREAT GLOBAL CLEANUP® MOVEMENT!
MONTCLAIR EARTH DAY EVENTS: 
APRIL 18, 10-noon:  

Open House at Pine Street Community Garden – come check out the garden and learn about the new solar-powered Rocket composter

APRIL 22, 7-9 pm: 

Movie screening: “The Little Things That Run the World” – part of the Bee City USA series.
Guild Room, First Congregational Church, Montclair

APRIL 25, 10-11:30 am: 

Crane Park Pollinator Garden clean up – help us get the garden ready for Spring, and see what’s already growing! 

APRIL 26, 12-4 pm:

Montclair Earth Fest
South Park St. between Bloomfield Ave. and Church St.


 
For additional information & volunteer opportunities, please e-mail us at: Info@neearth.org or check our calendar for other events and workshops. 

Make every day Earth Day

Fight climate change with diet change
Plant native plants
Stand against deforestation
Organize community cleanups
Compost
Reduce plastic use
Go pesticide free
Turn off lights when not in use
Save paper – switch to online billing
Support organic agriculture

title: Forest Regeneration Volunteer Workday
description:
South Mountain Conservancy Activities Meetup Group
Forest Regeneration activities focus on the areas in and around the fenced 14-acre Forest and Wildflower Preserve located near the Dog Park on Bear Lane in the Reservation. On workdays, we focus on removing invasive plants like knotweed, garlic mustard and mugwort. Ages 12 to 15 are welcome with a parent or guardian present. Ages 15 and above require a signature by a parent or guardian on a liability waiver; teenagers cannot just be dropped off.

Please arrive 10 to 15 minutes early so we can hike into the Wildflower Preserve at 9 am sharp.

Please dress appropriately! There is some poison ivy on trails and in work areas. Wear long pants, long-sleeved shirt, closed-toe shoes or rain boots, bug spray, and layer as needed. We supply tools. If you have gloves, bring them or you can borrow a pair from us. Bring your own water.

For information, contact program co-coordinators Rich Rockwell and Tom Pollard at forestregen@[somocon.org](http://somocon.org/)

To see what's blooming in the Wildflower Preserve, check out our [Wildflower Preserve](https://www.inaturalist.org/projects/south-mountain-conservancy-wildflower-preserve) project on iNaturalist project.

**Meet at the tool sheds at the Dog Park at 11 Bear Lane, Maplewood NJ.**

title: Movie screening: "The Little Things That Run the World" - Earth Week Celebration
location: Guild Room at First Congregational Church, 40 South Fullerton Avenue, Montclair, New Jersey, 07042, 07042, United States
description:
Join us for a screening of “The Little Things That Run the World,” part of our Bee City USA program. 
Synopsis:
Life on Earth is in the middle of a major re-alignment of species composition. The smallest of creatures – insects – hold the fabric of all life together. They are the most numerous of all animal groups on the planet. But insects are declining at an alarming rate throughout the world, threatening innumerable species, whole ecosystems, and even our food systems with collapse. This is story about untangling the mysteries as to why something so ubiquitous – and seemingly endless – could be on the cusp of disappearing entirely. It is also the story of heroic efforts to save these most vulnerable of creatures. 
Admission is free. Register to save your seat. 
Learn more about the film here.
